audits.dataflow:
IN: data/raw-wiktextract-data.jsonl (~23 GB English wiktextract), data/wiktionary/{ru,pl,cs}-extract.jsonl (native editions), data/official-isv.csv (official dictionary export).
OUT (each written by exactly one CLI command, gzip-with-.json-name via dump::write_gz, atomic tmp+rename, schema-stamped):
- extract-proto -> data/proto-slavic.cache.json (ProtoCache, schema 1); read by dump::ProtoIndex::load -> proto_link, eval, site/mod.rs:76,989.
- extract-lemmas -> data/slavic-lemmas.cache.json (LemmaCorpus, schema 1); read by site::export_corpus, eval, falsefriends, coincheck, site/coverage, extract-enrich, AND implicitly by ProtoIndex::load (dump.rs:1577 hardcodes crate::DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE).
- extract-raw-slavic -> data/raw-slavic-lemmas.cache.json (RawSlavicCorpus, schema 0) + sibling data/raw-slavic-coverage.json (plain JSON, RawCoverageStats); read by site/mod.rs:735, site/coverage.rs, coincheck, falsefriends, flavorize, and main.rs:387 (extract-enrich wanted-set union).
- extract-enrich -> data/wiktionary-enrich.cache.json (EnrichCache, schema 0); read by enrich::EnrichIndex::load -> site, falsefriends, site/coverage. Wanted set = enrich::build_wanted(LemmaCorpus + official cells + raw lemmas).
official::load parses the CSV via its own RFC-4180 read_csv_records (also reused by release.rs:210); model.rs supplies Pos::parse/parse_noun_traits consumed by official.rs and dump.rs. Load-time mutations (proto homoglyph folding dump.rs:255-260,1568-1570; enrich markup filter enrich.rs:134-144) happen at the single ingress, caches stay verbatim. No cache has two writers; the one nonlinear edge is ProtoIndex::load reading the lemma cache behind the caller's back.

findings.summary: english_gloss_tokens lives in site but is a check.rs dependency (3 call sites), coupling the checker to the site exporter
findings.evidence: english_api.rs:329 `pub fn english_gloss_tokens(gloss: &str) -> BTreeSet<String>`; consumed by check.rs:396 `gloss_tokens: crate::site::english_gloss_tokens(gloss)`, check.rs:530, check.rs:1067, and re-exported at site/mod.rs:2135. check.rs:303 doc even cites it: "normalizes gloss keys ([`crate::site::english_gloss_tokens`])"
findings.fix: Move english_gloss_tokens (with its gloss_keys/usable_token_key dependencies) into a neutral gloss module (glossxref.rs or new gloss.rs); keep a `pub use` in site so both site and check import the same symbol — pure relocation, byte-identical export

findings.summary: valence_char parses the official POS tag inside check.rs while model.rs owns that tag's grammar (Pos::parse, parse_noun_traits)
findings.evidence: check.rs:248-258 `fn valence_char(pos_raw: &str) -> char { let s = pos_raw.to_lowercase(); if s.contains(".intr") {'i'} else if s.contains(".refl") {'r'} else if s.contains(".tr") {'t'} ... }` — third parser over the same `pos_raw` string that model.rs:246 Pos::parse and model.rs:359 parse_noun_traits already dissect
findings.fix: Move it to model.rs beside parse_noun_traits as `pub fn verb_valence(raw: &str) -> char`; check.rs:125 calls the relocated function — same bytes in the export

findings.summary: ProtoIndex::load silently loads a SECOND cache (DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE) from a cwd-relative hardcoded path, ignoring the caller's path argument
findings.evidence: dump.rs:1576-1580 `if let Some(corpus) = load_optional(Path::new(crate::DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE), LemmaCorpus::load)? { idx.attach_etymology(&corpus); }` inside `ProtoIndex::load(path)` — the linker's etym/deep_etym maps appear or vanish depending on the process cwd, not on any explicit input; a caller passing a custom --proto-cache still reads data/slavic-lemmas.cache.json relative to cwd
findings.fix: Lift the coupling to callers: `ProtoIndex::load_with_lemmas(proto_path, lemma_path)` (or accept Option<&LemmaCorpus>), with existing call sites passing DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE explicitly — dependency becomes visible, behavior unchanged

findings.summary: extract-enrich collapses RawSlavicCorpus::load errors to an empty Vec, violating dump.rs's own documented load_optional contract
findings.evidence: main.rs:387-389 `RawSlavicCorpus::load(...).map(|c| c.lemmas).unwrap_or_default()` vs dump.rs:84-87 doc: "Callers must NOT collapse that error to `None` (`.ok()` / `.unwrap_or_default()`), or a stale cache silently degrades the output, which is exactly what the schema stamps exist to prevent." A corrupt/stale-schema raw cache silently shrinks the enrich wanted set. Same violation at coincheck.rs:292-293 (`.ok()` on both LemmaCorpus and RawSlavicCorpus loads)
findings.fix: Route through dump::load_optional so absent stays Ok(None) but corrupt/stale is a hard error (identical output when the cache is healthy, which is the frozen baseline)

findings.summary: string_values and push_limited are byte-identical copies of the dump.rs helpers; wiki_metadata is a near-copy differing only in caps
findings.evidence: enrich.rs:592-614 `fn string_values(v: Option<&Value>, cap: usize)` and 616-625 `fn push_limited` are character-for-character identical to dump.rs:895-917 and 919-928; enrich.rs:572-590 wiki_metadata mirrors dump.rs:876-893 (enrich adds top-level raw_tags and uses caps 28/32 vs 24/28)
findings.fix: Survivor: the dump.rs pair promoted to pub(crate) (or a small shared wikijson helper module); enrich.rs imports them and keeps its own wiki_metadata caps — identical output

findings.summary: Four hand-rolled copies of the same dump-streaming skeleton (open/8MB BufReader/lines/substring prefilter/serde parse/lang_code re-verify)
findings.evidence: dump.rs:624-709 extract_lemmas, dump.rs:1092-1158 extract_raw_slavic, dump.rs:1341-1373 extract, enrich.rs:351-407 enrich::extract all repeat `BufReader::with_capacity(8 * 1024 * 1024, file)`, `for line in reader.lines()`, a `line.contains(marker)` prefilter, `serde_json::from_str` with `Err(_) => continue`, and a lang_code re-check
findings.fix: One `stream_jsonl(path, prefilter: impl Fn(&str)->bool, f: impl FnMut(&Value))` helper in dump.rs consumed by all four extractors; per-pass logic stays in the closures, cache bytes unchanged

findings.summary: Cache-load boilerplate (read_maybe_gz + parse + check_cache_schema + entry_count ensure) copy-pasted three times
findings.evidence: LemmaCorpus::load dump.rs:232-249, ProtoIndex::load dump.rs:1546-1563, EnrichIndex::load enrich.rs:111-130 each repeat the same four steps including the identical `entry_count {} but {} entries` ensure message shape
findings.fix: Generic `load_stamped_cache<T>(kind, path, expected_schema, regen)` beside check_cache_schema returning the parsed struct; the three loaders keep only their post-load hygiene passes

findings.summary: extract_lemmas' prefilter hardcodes every old-stage code as string literals, duplicating OLD_STAGE_LANGS — adding a stage to the map silently misses the prefilter
findings.evidence: dump.rs:647-661 `line.contains("zlw-opl") || line.contains("zlw-ocs") || line.contains("zlw-osk") || line.contains("zle-ort") || ...` repeats each member of OLD_STAGE_LANGS (dump.rs:434-436); a new entry in OLD_STAGE_OF without a matching literal here drops the whole evidence class with no error
findings.fix: Build the marker list once from OLD_STAGE_LANGS (quoted/colon forms, keeping the documented special-casing for short codes orv/cu) — same set, byte-identical cache

findings.summary: lemma_gloss and real_glosses/is_form_of_sense are two parallel skip-form-of-sense implementations in the same file with diverging tag sets
findings.evidence: lemma_gloss dump.rs:1050-1078 checks `sense.get("form_of").is_some()` and tags `form-of|inflection-of`, caps at 80 chars; real_glosses/is_form_of_sense dump.rs:1278-1328 checks non-empty form_of/alt_of arrays plus tags `form-of|inflection-of|alt-of|alternative|abbreviation` and gloss_marks_form_of, caps at 200
findings.fix: Co-locate the two and document the divergence; unify only after a truth-table proof over the dump (the tag sets differ, so blind merging changes cache bytes) — the safe move now is extracting the shared tag-scan into one helper parameterized by tag set

findings.summary: Pos::parse repeats `s.starts_with("m.")` twice in one condition and re-implements parse_noun_traits' m./f./n. prefix grammar
findings.evidence: model.rs:304-312: `if s.starts_with("m.") || s.starts_with("f.") || ... || s.starts_with("m/") || s.starts_with("m.")` — the final clause is a literal duplicate of the first; the same gender-prefix grammar lives again in parse_noun_traits model.rs:362-367 (which additionally accepts "m ")
findings.fix: Delete the duplicate clause (provably identical); co-locate the two tag parsers and note the "m " asymmetry so the next edit touches one grammar, not two

findings.summary: enrich::truncate duplicates dump::truncate_chars (char-cap + ellipsis)
findings.evidence: enrich.rs:658-665 `fn truncate(s: &str, max_chars: usize)` and dump.rs:868-874 `fn truncate_chars(s: &str, max_chars: usize)` both count chars and append '…' — same output for all inputs
findings.fix: Survivor: dump.rs truncate_chars made pub(crate) (or moved to normalize.rs); enrich.rs imports it

findings.summary: chain_key duplicates enrich::norm_word (trim + strip U+0300–036F combining marks + lowercase)
findings.evidence: dump.rs:449-455 `word.trim().chars().filter(|c| !('\u{0300}'..='\u{036F}').contains(c)).flat_map(char::to_lowercase)` vs enrich.rs:179-185 `word.trim().to_lowercase().chars().filter(|c| !('\u{0300}'..='\u{036F}').contains(c))` — order differs but to_lowercase never emits combining marks, so results are equal
findings.fix: One accent-fold helper in normalize.rs used by both (keep both call-site names as thin wrappers if the diff must stay minimal)

findings.summary: Confidence::from_score uses magic literals 0.72/0.45 while its sibling from_probability uses named calibrate consts; the numbers are also hardcoded in eval.rs prose
findings.evidence: model.rs:51-53 `if score >= 0.72 ... else if score >= 0.45`; contrast model.rs:65-67 `if p >= crate::calibrate::PROPOSE_T`; eval.rs:3406 embeds "thresholds 0.72/0.45 in `Confidence::from_score`" in a generated report string — doc-rot magnet if either literal moves
findings.fix: Name them (e.g. SCORE_HIGH_T / SCORE_MEDIUM_T in model.rs or calibrate.rs) and reference the consts from the eval report text

findings.summary: The TSV branch of official::load (looks_like_tsv) has no in-repo caller and uses quote-blind split beside the real CSV parser
findings.evidence: official.rs:230-231 `fn looks_like_tsv(header: &str) -> bool { header.contains('\t') && !header.starts_with("id,") }` and 239-242 naive `l.split('\t')`; every caller (check.rs:1639,1937, coincheck.rs:253, derive.rs:437, release.rs:197-198, site) passes the comma CSV official-isv.csv; no .tsv file or TSV-producing target exists in repo/Makefile
findings.fix: Delete the branch (or demote to a documented escape hatch) once confirmed no out-of-repo workflow feeds a TSV; read_csv_records is the survivor

stale_candidates: src/official.rs:134 OfficialEntry::native_branches — pub method whose only callers are its own unit tests (official.rs:361); no production use found repo-wide
src/official.rs:230 looks_like_tsv + the tab branch of load() — no caller ever passes a TSV (all pass official-isv.csv); likely leftover from an earlier metadata-TSV workflow

src/main.rs:370 site::export(&official, &out) fallback (Export when DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE is absent) — production/CI always has the committed lemma cache, so the official-only site path may be an unexercised second exporter (verify before touching; outside my four files)

src/dump.rs pre-stamp schema-0 tolerance narrative (dump.rs:61-79 comment block) — partially superseded: LEMMA_CACHE_SCHEMA moved to 1 so schema-0 caches are now refused, but the comment still explains why 0 was 'deliberately the initial expected value'; doc-rot candidate
src/enrich.rs:159-165 EnrichIndex::len/is_empty — len is used (site/coverage), is_empty not found outside the file; harmless clippy-pair but unused

audits.dataflow:
IN: data/slavic-lemmas.cache.json (dump::LemmaCorpus), data/proto-slavic.cache.json (dump::ProtoIndex), data/official-isv.csv (official::load).
SITE path (export_corpus, the shipped export): LemmaCorpus -> corpus::build_sets (union-find cognate sets; prints a stats line) -> corpus::generate_set = consensus::generate(ConsensusConfig::production) + proto::generate_with_reflexes(set.proto, stem_class=None — the known Wiktionary ancestor; proto_link is NEVER called on this path) -> GeneratedWord consumed by site/mod.rs:317/408, site/coverage.rs:206/215, site/entries.rs, site/special.rs. derive::pattern_probabilities(official) (site/mod.rs:1811) and derive::derive_family (site/entries.rs:812, site/coverage.rs:1406) feed shipped derivative pages. site/coverage.rs:725 borrowings path calls pipeline::generate(input, proto=None).
EVAL/fallback path: pipeline::generate(_oracle) = consensus::generate_oracle + proto_link::{link_explicit, link, link_oracle} + proto::generate_with_reflexes(stem_class from cfg.proto_stem_class_endings) — used by eval.rs (headline ladder, oracle/select-eval/rep-eval), aspect.rs, generator.rs, and the official-seeded fallback site::export.
CROSS-COMMAND ARTIFACT: `corpus-eval --fit` (eval::run_corpus_eval) re-drives corpus::build_sets + generate_set and writes data/corpus-calibration.json, which `export` reads back (site/mod.rs:469) — the one generation artifact produced by one command and consumed by another.
EVAL-ONLY code living inside generation modules: consensus::Oracle + forced-cluster/oracle-rep branches, pick_rep_by_rule("modal-skeleton"/"shortest"), proto_link::link_oracle, the deep-corroboration rescue (RESCUE_FLOOR/deep_share), REP_PRIORITY_NO_SOUTH_BIAS + REP_PRIORITY_ADJ, ConsensusConfig::baseline(), tidy_ending, palatal_from_south/jat_reconstruction/recover_y — all reachable only via eval subcommands (kept alive by the ablation/rejected ladders). ConsensusConfig::full() is reachable from nowhere.

findings.summary: corpus::generate_set and pipeline::generate_oracle are drifted copy-paste twins of the same post-processing: insert proto candidate first, append reflexive ' sę', dedupe by standard spelling preferring the proto-flavored form
findings.evidence: corpus.rs:531-534 appends the particle with only `!pc.form.ends_with(" sę")`, while pipeline.rs:232-238 first strips residual particles [" sę"," sa"," se"," sie"," się"," sobě"]; corpus::dedupe (613-632) silently drops the unflavored duplicate while pipeline::dedupe (253-288) upgrades it in place ('existing.form = c.form.clone(); existing.source = CandidateSource::ProtoSlavicRule'). Both key on `ortho::to_standard(&c.form.to_lowercase())`. Note pipeline's strip list also omits " sobe" that consensus::reflexive_stem (consensus.rs:1618) handles — three drifted particle lists total.
findings.fix: Extract shared post-processing helpers (append_reflexive_particle, dedupe_by_standard_spelling with an upgrade-vs-drop flag) into one module, each caller keeping its exact current particle list and dedupe policy; then unify lists only with a measured-neutral proof. Survivor: pipeline.rs as the host.

findings.summary: ConsensusConfig::full() is dead — no caller anywhere in the crate (tests included)
findings.evidence: grep for 'full()' across src/ hits only the definition at consensus.rs:229 and two unrelated `rows[i].full()` calls on a different type in site/search.rs:371/395. The ablation ladder (eval.rs kept_ladder) rebuilds its top rung field-by-field and rejected_experiments() starts from production(); neither uses full().
findings.fix: Delete ConsensusConfig::full(). Byte-identical trivially (dead code).

findings.summary: generate_set silently ignores the ConsensusConfig gates it receives: the proto-derivation block runs unconditionally and stem_class is hard-coded None while production() says proto_stem_class_endings=true
findings.evidence: corpus.rs:519 `if !set.borrowed {` is not gated on cfg.proto_derived_form; corpus.rs:527-530: 'stem_class stays None on the site path: site output must not change until the display side ships its own readers (issue #76)' followed by `generate_with_reflexes(proto_word, set.pos, None, &reflexes, None)`. Meanwhile pipeline.rs:84-88 does honor the flag. A production() flag flip never reaches the site path.
findings.fix: Split the fields generate_set actually honors into a narrower config (or take no ConsensusConfig for the proto block and document the site-frozen knobs at the ConsensusConfig fields), so the two-stack divergence is typed instead of implicit. Pure signature refactor, output unchanged.

findings.summary: corpus::debase_stress hand-duplicates proto::debase_vowel, the table that was made pub(crate) precisely 'so the two sides can never drift'
findings.evidence: corpus.rs:373-383 maps the same accented-vowel sets to a/e/i/o/u/y as proto.rs:602-617; proto.rs:600-601 doc: 'the site's proto-reflex join (issue #73b) folds ancestor and cache words with this same table, so the two sides can never drift.' Only delta: debase_vowel also maps U+1EF9 ỹ, and `grep -c 'ỹ' data/slavic-lemmas.cache.json` finds 0 occurrences, so proto_merge_key output is byte-identical on the committed corpus.
findings.fix: Delete debase_stress; call crate::proto::debase_vowel from proto_merge_key. Prove with export hash (ỹ absent from the cache).

findings.summary: Answer-reading diagnostic machinery (Oracle, forced-cluster reorder, oracle-representative pick, rep rules 'modal-skeleton'/'shortest') lives inside the production consensus module
findings.evidence: consensus.rs:284-304 `pub struct Oracle` ('Every field READS THE OFFICIAL ANSWER and must never feed production'); reconstruct() branches on it at 628-645; generate_oracle's forced_key block at 389-399; proto_link.rs:84-134 link_oracle ('READS THE ANSWER'). Callers are exclusively eval.rs (993, 1186, 2355 via pipeline::generate_oracle) behind the Oracle/SelectEval/RepEval subcommands.
findings.fix: Move Oracle + link_oracle + the non-production pick_rep_by_rule arms into an eval-side module (production reconstruct keeps only the medoid arm), leaving generate() a thin oracle-free path. Pure code motion, byte-identical.

findings.summary: derive.rs mixes the generation layer (derive_family, pattern_probabilities — used by the site) with a 300-line benchmark writer that reaches into eval and calibrate internals
findings.evidence: run_eval (436-632) writes target/eval/derivation-report.md and, like holdout_pattern_stats, calls crate::eval::is_holdout_id (344, 467, 481) and crate::calibrate::REVIEW_T (426); naive_family (81-127) exists only as the benchmark baseline. The site needs only derive_family (site/entries.rs:812) and pattern_probabilities (site/mod.rs:1811).
findings.fix: Move run_eval + naive_family + the report writer next to the other benchmarks in eval.rs (or a derive_eval module), keeping mine_pairs/holdout_pattern_stats with pattern_probabilities. Code motion only.

findings.summary: Candidate-collection loop (gloss_candidates ∪ desc_candidates with contains-dedup) is duplicated between link_core and link_oracle
findings.evidence: proto_link.rs:216-225 and 90-103 are the same union: `let mut candidates: Vec<usize> = index.gloss_candidates(...); ... if let Some(v) = index.desc_candidates(sk) { for &i in v { if !candidates.contains(&i) { candidates.push(i); } } }` — the only difference is link_oracle recomputes each form's skeleton inline.
findings.fix: Extract fn collect_candidates(index, gloss, skeletons) -> Vec<usize>; both callers keep their exact iteration order (gloss first, then per-skeleton) so the Vec order — and therefore tie-breaks — is unchanged.

findings.summary: corpus::branch_of is a pure delegation shim to crate::lang::branch_of that external modules still route through
findings.evidence: corpus.rs:59-61: `pub fn branch_of(lang: &str) -> Option<Branch> { crate::lang::branch_of(lang) }` with a doc admitting the old local table 'had already drifted'. Callers: site/navigation.rs:101, site/entries.rs:901, site/special.rs:456 all call crate::corpus::branch_of.
findings.fix: Point all callers at crate::lang::branch_of and delete the shim. Survivor: lang::branch_of. Byte-identical trivially.

findings.summary: PREFIX_VARIANTS contains the literal entry ("voz", "vȯz") twice; the second is unreachable
findings.evidence: proto_link.rs:343-344: `("voz", "vȯz"),\n    ("voz", "vȯz"),` — strip_one iterates in order and returns on the first strip_prefix match, so the duplicate can never fire.
findings.fix: Delete the duplicate row. Byte-identical (unreachable entry).

findings.summary: inverse_palatalization/inverse_iotation are one-line leftover wrappers around interslavic::phono from the issue-#21 migration
findings.evidence: derive.rs:135-143: `fn inverse_palatalization(stem: &str) -> Vec<String> { interslavic::phono::inverse_palatalization(stem) }` and the identical-shape inverse_iotation; both called only inside mine_pairs (222, 249, 261, 276).
findings.fix: Call interslavic::phono directly at the four call sites and delete the wrappers.

findings.summary: round3 is duplicated verbatim in pipeline.rs and consensus.rs
findings.evidence: pipeline.rs:313-315 and consensus.rs:1493-1495 are byte-identical: `fn round3(x: f32) -> f32 { (x * 1000.0).round() / 1000.0 }`.
findings.fix: One pub(crate) round3 (natural home: model.rs next to Candidate scoring, or orthography.rs); survivor consensus's, pipeline imports it.

findings.summary: tidy_ending's Noun arm is a no-op branch (empty body behind a condition), and the whole function is reachable only on the eval baseline rung
findings.evidence: consensus.rs:1479-1483: `Pos::Noun => { if gender == Some(Gender::Neuter) && !ends_with_vowel(&w) { // leave; neuter usually already ends in -o/-e } }` — the if-body is empty; ends_with_vowel (1489) exists only for it. tidy_ending is called only when internationalism, lemma_endings AND prefix_normalization are all false (778/794), i.e. ConsensusConfig::baseline() — never production, never the site.
findings.fix: Delete the Noun arm and ends_with_vowel (provable identity); optionally fold tidy_ending toward the eval-only baseline rung it serves.

findings.summary: build_sets prints its borrowing-layer stats via println! from a library function, so every consumer (export, coverage, corpus-eval --fit) emits it, and the translit_keyed/bridge_folds accounting exists only to feed that stdout line
findings.evidence: corpus.rs:215-229 `println!("borrowing layer: {} lemmas → {} components ...")` inside pub fn build_sets; counters translit_keyed (93), bridge_folds (191) and the comps_before/after machinery (178-198) are consumed nowhere else. Called from site/mod.rs:317, site/coverage.rs:206 and eval.rs:318.
findings.fix: Return a BuildStats struct alongside the sets (or accept a reporting sink) and let main-path callers print it; stdout text can be kept byte-identical at the call sites while the library becomes silent.

findings.summary: REP_PRIORITY_NO_SOUTH_BIAS and REP_PRIORITY_ADJ are generation tables kept alive solely by eval ladder rungs (baseline / rejected adj_longform_rep experiment)
findings.evidence: consensus.rs:615 selects REP_PRIORITY_NO_SOUTH_BIAS only when prefer_south_representative=false — true only in ConsensusConfig::baseline() (eval.rs:35 kept_ladder rung 1); consensus.rs:611 selects REP_PRIORITY_ADJ only under adj_longform_rep, which production() sets false ('Rejected by the benchmark') and only eval.rs:134's rejected-experiments rung enables. Same story for palatal_from_south/jat_reconstruction/recover_y and proto_link's RESCUE_FLOOR/deep_share (production comment: 'fired on exactly 1 of 16,300 meanings ... +0.00pp').
findings.fix: No deletion (the ablation/rejected ladders still execute them) — but tag each with a '// eval-only: kept for the ablation ladder' marker or group them in an eval_rungs section, so a future ladder retirement can remove them mechanically. Documentation-only, byte-identical.

stale_candidates: ConsensusConfig::full() (src/consensus.rs:229) — proven dead: zero callers crate-wide; also listed as a finding

proto_link deep-corroboration rescue (RESCUE_FLOOR/RESCUE_SHARE/deep_share, src/proto_link.rs:41-42, 291-327) — production-off; reachable only through the 'prod+link-corroboration' rejected-experiments rung whose own description says it fired on 1/16,300 meanings and moved +0.00pp; dead the moment that rung is retired

consensus::Oracle.cluster / force_cluster_key / rep_rule branches and pick_rep_by_rule('modal-skeleton'|'shortest') — alive only via the Oracle/SelectEval/RepEval diagnostic subcommands; if those measurement campaigns are considered concluded (their headline numbers are already baked into rung descriptions), this is a removable diagnostic layer
consensus::tidy_ending + ends_with_vowel (consensus.rs:1455-1491) — reachable only on the baseline eval rung (all ending flags false); never runs for the site or production config
REP_PRIORITY_NO_SOUTH_BIAS and REP_PRIORITY_ADJ (consensus.rs:270-277) — selected only under eval-only configs (baseline / rejected adj_longform_rep)

consensus.rs palatal_from_south (1243), jat_reconstruction (1349), recover_y (1383) — production flags false ('Rejected by the benchmark'); exercised only by rejected_experiments() and full() (itself dead)
corpus.rs seen_lang: BTreeMap<&str, bool> used as a set (corpus.rs:454) — not stale but an improper micro; BTreeSet expresses it
proto.rs generate() (the reflex-less wrapper, proto.rs:17-19) — NOT stale (proto_link scoring and eval use it) but easily mistaken for it; site path only ever calls generate_with_reflexes

audits.area: surface — string/orthography/morphology machinery (flavorize.rs, morph.rs, normalize.rs, lang.rs, aspect.rs, glossxref.rs, thesaurus.rs, plus orthography.rs/model.rs/check.rs cross-checks)
audits.dataflow:
In: official CSV cells (pos_raw, per-language translation cells, English glosses), Wiktionary lemma caches, raw candidate strings.
- lang.rs is the static registry (branch/script/speakers/pop_weight); consumed by normalize dispatch, consensus weighting, razumlivost, and site display. thesaurus.rs re-hardcodes a subset of it.
- normalize.rs turns raw cells into NormForm{original, phonemic-Latin, ascii skeleton} for the consensus vote; also exports translit_greek (corpus etymon keys), fold_proto_homoglyphs (proto engine), fold_deep_token, desc_skeleton (proto_link matching).
- orthography.rs re-exports interslavic::to_standard and layers ascii_skeleton → consonant_key → shares_consonant_root plus edit distances; virtually every module folds through it, often re-inlining the `to_standard(&x.trim().to_lowercase())` idiom that forms::form_key already names.
- morph.rs (LemmaRules, benchmark-gated) rewrites candidate lemma endings for consensus.rs only.
- aspect.rs parses the official pos_raw aspect tag, detect_pairs defines the frozen 1,440-pair benchmark slice consumed by eval.rs and site/mod.rs; generate_pair reaches UP into pipeline::generate (inverted layering).
- flavorize.rs is display-only (site/coverage.rs, site/entries.rs, coincheck.rs, falsefriends.rs); its header forbids leaking into voting — the parallel Cyrillic tables vs normalize.rs are deliberate, documented duplication.
- glossxref.rs and thesaurus.rs are export-time in-memory indexes (never persisted) feeding site/entries.rs and synonym-aware scoring; both depend on foreign tokenizers (dump::gloss_tokens, site::english_gloss_tokens) — the crate has five gloss tokenizers total.
Out: normalized forms into consensus/pipeline, aspect pairs into eval + site artifacts, flavorized headwords into site HTML, gloss/synonym links into site pages. No artifact in this area is written by two commands.

findings.summary: Four independent substring parsers of the one official pos_raw CSV column, scattered across four modules
findings.evidence: aspect.rs:31-41 `pub fn aspect(pos_raw: &str)` matches `contains("ipf./pf.")/contains("ipf.")/contains("pf.")`; check.rs:248-259 `fn valence_char(pos_raw: &str)` matches `.intr/.refl/.tr`; model.rs:246-316 `Pos::parse` + model.rs:359-374 `parse_noun_traits` match gender/number prefixes (`m.`, `f.pl.`, `indecl`); eval.rs:1909-1913 re-inlines `e.pos_raw.contains("ipf.")` / `contains("pf.")` instead of calling aspect::aspect, duplicating detect_pairs' grouping preamble (aspect.rs:55-71) verbatim including the biaspectual-on-ipf-side quirk.
findings.fix: Create one pos-tag module (e.g. src/postag.rs) exposing aspect(), valence(), traits() with the EXACT current substring semantics moved verbatim; point eval.rs:1909 at aspect::aspect (identical truth table: ipf./pf. rows land on the ipf side in both). Byte-identity provable because every matcher is a pure function of pos_raw and the bodies move unchanged.

findings.summary: forms::form_key body is re-implemented verbatim as thesaurus::key and hand-inlined at ~15 more call sites
findings.evidence: forms.rs:119-121 `pub fn form_key(form:&str)->String{ortho::to_standard(&form.trim().to_lowercase())}`; thesaurus.rs:53-55 `fn key(isv:&str)->String{ortho::to_standard(&isv.trim().to_lowercase())}` is character-identical; grep shows the same idiom inlined in derive.rs:163,169,303-304, inflect_eval.rs:20, proto_link.rs:89, pipeline.rs:262,268,301, eval.rs:649, corpus.rs:624, orthography.rs:107.
findings.fix: Hoist the helper into orthography.rs (e.g. `pub fn fold_key`) with forms::form_key as a re-export, delete thesaurus::key, and replace the inlined idiom call-by-call — each site is a textual substitution of an identical expression, so the export is byte-identical by construction.

findings.summary: Triple-fold: callers wrap ortho::consonant_key in an extra to_standard+lowercase it already performs
findings.evidence: aspect.rs:97-99 `fn consonant_key(word:&str)->String{ortho::consonant_key(&ortho::to_standard(&word.to_lowercase()))}`; same pattern at pipeline.rs:73 and eval.rs:635. But orthography.rs:59-61 consonant_key starts with `let skel = ascii_skeleton(word);` and ascii_skeleton (orthography.rs:27) is `to_standard(&word.to_lowercase())` — so the outer fold is applied twice.
findings.fix: Replace with `ortho::consonant_key(word)` after adding a to_standard-idempotence property test (to_standard(to_standard(x))==to_standard(x), and lowercase-stability) pinned over the official lexicon; the interslavic crate's fold maps into the standard alphabet whose letters are fixed points, so removal is provably byte-identical.

findings.summary: Provably unreachable/duplicate suffix-table entries in international_ending and verb_infinitive
findings.evidence: morph.rs:391-395: verb list `["ovati", "ować", "ovať", "ovat", " irovati", "irovať", "izovati", ...]` is guarded by `word.ends_with(suf) && !word.ends_with("ovati")` (line 395) — any word ending in "ovati", " irovati" or "izovati" also ends in "ovati", so those three entries can never fire. morph.rs:274 `for suf in ["ično", "ično", "ičny", ...]` lists "ično" twice. morph.rs:265 `" yczny", "yczny"` and morph.rs:449 `(" nąć", "nuti"), ("nąć", "nuti")`: the leading-space variant maps to the same replacement as its space-free twin, which matches everything the spaced one matches (the space just lands in the retained stem) — identical output either way.
findings.fix: Delete the dead entries ("ovati", " irovati", "izovati", duplicate "ično", " yczny", " nąć"); each removal is provably byte-identical by the guard/subsumption argument above. Add a unit test asserting derive on e.g. "organizovati" is unchanged.

findings.summary: The combining-mark predicate ('\u{0300}'..='\u{036F}').contains(c) is hand-inlined six times across two modules
findings.evidence: flavorize.rs:92-96 strip_marks, flavorize.rs:729 ru_text, flavorize.rs:810 previous_base; normalize.rs:129 translit_greek, normalize.rs:221 fold_deep_token, normalize.rs:457 desc_skeleton — all six filter on the identical range expression.
findings.fix: Add `pub fn is_combining_mark(c: char) -> bool` to orthography.rs and substitute at all six sites; pure predicate extraction, byte-identical.

findings.summary: Five parallel English gloss tokenizers, with check.rs reaching into site for one of them
findings.evidence: glossxref.rs:134 head_tokens (own STOP list, 2..=32 chars, " of " filter); dump.rs:1685 gloss_tokens (own STOP, len>=3, Vec); falsefriends.rs:182 gloss_tokens (BTreeSet, light_stem) plus falsefriends.rs:199 ordered_tokens duplicating its body; site/english_api.rs:329 english_gloss_tokens; check.rs:396 and check.rs:530,1067 call `crate::site::english_gloss_tokens` — a check-side gate depending on a site-side helper (the known check<->site offender), and thesaurus.rs:91 calls `crate::dump::gloss_tokens` (an export index depending on the dump loader for tokenization).
findings.fix: Create src/gloss.rs and MOVE all five tokenizers verbatim under distinct names (dump_tokens, ff_tokens/ff_ordered, english_api_tokens, xref_head_tokens) — no behavior merging, imports rewritten only. Removes the check→site and thesaurus→dump reaches; byte-identical because bodies are relocated unchanged.

findings.summary: aspect.rs (leaf morphology module) reaches up into pipeline::generate, inverting the layering
findings.evidence: aspect.rs:183-190: `let ipf = crate::pipeline::generate(imperfective, proto, consensus_cfg).0.into_iter().next()?; let pf = crate::pipeline::generate(perfective, ...)` inside generate_pair — the only upward dependency in the module; everything else in aspect.rs is pure string morphology consumed BY eval.rs and site/mod.rs.
findings.fix: Move generate_pair (aspect.rs:176-192) verbatim into pipeline.rs (it is an orchestration entry point: it runs the full generator twice, then calls aspect::reconcile_pair); aspect.rs keeps reconcile_pair/derive_* as pure functions. Call-site rename only, byte-identical.

findings.summary: Hard-coded SLAV language list duplicates the lang.rs registry (modern CSV-backed columns)
findings.evidence: thesaurus.rs:19-21 `const SLAV: &[&str] = &["ru","be","uk","pl","cs","sk","sl","hr","sr","bg","mk"]` — exactly lang.rs official_slavic_cols() (lang.rs:270-273) filtered to `modern` (drops cu), but with bg/mk in swapped order vs LANGS. The list only keys `by_trans: HashMap<(String,String), BTreeSet<...>>` (thesaurus.rs:80), so iteration order cannot affect output.
findings.fix: Derive it: `lang::official_slavic_cols().iter().filter(|l| l.modern).map(|l| l.csv_col)`. Neutrality provable because every (lang, trans-key) bucket ends in order-insensitive HashMap/BTreeSet containers; add a test asserting the derived set equals the old literal.

findings.summary: HashMap bracket-indexing in Thesaurus::build library path (plus per-pair key clones in the O(m²) loop)
findings.evidence: thesaurus.rs:128-131 `let (ia, ib) = (&info[&(*row_a, key_a.clone())], &info[&(*row_b, key_b.clone())]);` and thesaurus.rs:147 `isv: lemma_orig[&k].clone()` — panics if the invariant (every by_trans member was inserted into info/lemma_orig) is ever broken by a future edit; also allocates two String clones per candidate pair inside the nested i/j loop.
findings.fix: Replace with `.get(...).expect("by_trans member missing from info")` carrying the invariant message, or restructure members to carry indices into a Vec<Info>; same output, panic converted to a documented invariant and the clones removed.

findings.summary: Pos::parse repeats `s.starts_with("m.")` twice in one condition and drifts from parse_noun_traits' gender prefixes
findings.evidence: model.rs:304-312: `if s.starts_with("m.") || s.starts_with("f.") || s.starts_with("n.") || s == "m" || s == "f" || s == "n" || s.starts_with("m/") || s.starts_with("m.")` — the last disjunct duplicates the first. parse_noun_traits (model.rs:362) additionally accepts `s.starts_with("m ")` for masculine but has no `"f "`/`"n "` counterparts — two adjacent functions parsing the same prefixes with silently different accept sets.
findings.fix: Delete the duplicated `s.starts_with("m.")` disjunct (pure boolean identity, byte-identical) and extract one `gender_prefix(&str)->Option<Gender>` used by both, preserving each caller's exact accept set behind explicit flags until a behavior-change PR reconciles them.

findings.summary: The closed hard -tel word list is duplicated in derivational_suffixes and noun_lemma
findings.evidence: morph.rs:160 `let hard_tel = ["hotel", "kotel", "kostel", "dětel"];` and morph.rs:532-534 `!(["hotel", "kotel", "kostel", "dětel"].iter().any(|x| word.ends_with(x)))` — same closed set, two literals (note one checks contains, the other ends_with; keep both predicates, share the const).
findings.fix: Hoist `const HARD_TEL: [&str; 4]` to module scope and reference it from both sites; literal deduplication, byte-identical.

findings.summary: glossxref declares the rendering caps but site/entries.rs enforces two of the three
findings.evidence: glossxref.rs:17-21 declares `pub const MAX_LANGS`, `pub const MAX_PER_LANG`, `MAX_TOKENS`; MAX_TOKENS is enforced inside GlossXref::matches (glossxref.rs:117 `if out.len() >= MAX_TOKENS`), but MAX_LANGS/MAX_PER_LANG are applied only in site/entries.rs:657-660 (`.take(crate::glossxref::MAX_LANGS)`, `.take(crate::glossxref::MAX_PER_LANG)`) — the truncation policy is split across the module boundary.
findings.fix: Either move all three caps' enforcement into GlossXref::matches (returning already-truncated groups) or move the two display caps to site/entries.rs where they are applied; same .take() arithmetic, byte-identical HTML.

findings.summary: No-op match arm and dead stoplist character: micro-residue in normalize.rs (+ trivial is_letter wrapper in morph.rs)
findings.evidence: normalize.rs:397-398 `"sl" | "hr" | "bs" | "sh" => {} _ => {}` — the named arm is behaviorally identical to the fallthrough (documentation only). normalize.rs:377 `const CYR_VOWELS: &str = "аеёиіїоуыэюяєѣѫѧ "` carries a trailing space that can never match because line 378 requires `prev.is_alphabetic()`. morph.rs:629-631 `fn is_letter(c: char) -> bool { c.is_alphabetic() }` is a single-caller alias.
findings.fix: Fold the named arm into a comment on the `_` arm, drop the trailing space, inline is_letter into is_consonant — each is a provable no-op removal.

stale_candidates: src/glossxref.rs:124 GlossXref::token_count — pub fn with no callers found anywhere in src/ (only the definition matches); likely dead API
src/morph.rs:391-394 verb suffix entries "ovati", " irovati", "izovati" — proven unreachable (see finding 4), listed here as the concrete deletions

src/eval.rs:1900-1914 — re-implements aspect::detect_pairs' entry filtering and ipf/pf grouping inline; suspected copy that should call the aspect module (covered under finding 1 but the whole block may be collapsible)
src/lang.rs:15-21 Branch::code — could not confirm a caller in this pass (`.code()` hits were all Pos/declension codes); verify before removal

src/flavorize.rs:83-89 translit_text non-ru arms — pass-through is a documented placeholder for issue #38, not dead, but the function is a one-language dispatcher that will look vestigial until #38 lands

src/normalize.rs vs src/flavorize.rs parallel Cyrillic tables (translit_cyrillic vs cyr_word/bcms_cyr_to_latin/ru_text) — deliberate, documented duplication (flavorize header forbids leaking into voting); NOT a merge candidate, but the shared invariant consonant rows are copy-paste that will drift without a cross-pinning test

audits.dataflow:
In: data/official-isv.csv (official::load), data/novel-words.tsv (written by the site export, site/mod.rs:1622), data/project-lexicon-fixture.tsv / user --lexicon TSVs, false-friend caches (falsefriends::compute_from_default_caches; coincheck also loads DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE + DEFAULT_RAW_LEMMA_CACHE), interslavic crate paradigm tables.
Out: check-text terminal/JSON envelope (CHECKTEXT_JSON_SCHEMA=1); target/eval/checktext-report.md (check::run_eval); api/suggest/<n>.json + api/suggest-selftest.json (check::write_web_suggestions, invoked by site export at site/mod.rs:2032); api/forms/<n>.json, api/lemmas.json, api/meta.json, api/router-selftest.json, api/agent-guide.md (forms::write_api + forms::agent_guide, invoked at site/mod.rs:2023-2043); coin-check terminal/JSON + validated lexicon TSV row; data/MANIFEST.json (release::run_manifest, hashing git-tracked data/ files + Cargo.lock pin + site::PROBE_BASELINE + forms::SCHEMA_VERSION); data/official-isv.csv install + data/refresh-changelog.md prepend (release::run_refresh).
Cross-module touches: check→site (english_gloss_tokens: check.rs:396,530,1067); site→check (build_index at site/mod.rs:2029, write_web_suggestions, masc_animate_lemma_keys at site/mod.rs:1891); site→forms (write_api, agent_guide); release→site (PROBE_BASELINE at release.rs:134) and release→official (read_csv_records at release.rs:210).
Nonlinear hotspot: the export command writes data/novel-words.tsv mid-run (site/mod.rs:1622) and re-reads it from disk later in the same run (site/mod.rs:2029); check-text, checktext-eval and coin-check consume the committed copy via hardcoded string literals.

findings.summary: check.rs core validation depends on crate::site::english_gloss_tokens — gloss normalization lives in the site module but serves the checker's lexicon/adoption/consistency logic
findings.evidence: check.rs:396 `gloss_tokens: crate::site::english_gloss_tokens(gloss)` (parse_lexicon), :530 `let proposal_tokens = crate::site::english_gloss_tokens(&proposal.gloss)` (validate_lexicon_row adoption guard), :1067 `.map(|r| crate::site::english_gloss_tokens(&r.gloss))` (consistency_warning). Defined at src/site/english_api.rs:329 `pub fn english_gloss_tokens(gloss: &str) -> BTreeSet<String>`. The known offender, confirmed at three call sites.
findings.fix: Move english_gloss_tokens into a neutral shared module (normalize.rs or a new gloss.rs); re-export from site::english_api so the English API path is untouched. Pure move — byte-identical output.

findings.summary: data/novel-words.tsv is written by the export into the source data/ dir mid-run, re-read from disk later in the same run, and parsed by three hand-rolled readers with no shared schema or path constant
findings.evidence: Writer: site/mod.rs:1622 `std::fs::write("data/novel-words.tsv", &tsv)?` (10-column writeln at :1606-1618); same run re-reads it at site/mod.rs:2029-2031 `build_index(..., Some(Path::new("data/novel-words.tsv")), ...)`. Readers hand-parse with magic indices: check.rs:179-214 (`cols.len() < 8`, `(cols[0], cols[1], cols[2], cols[7])`), duplicated in test check.rs:2629-2639, plus hardcoded literals at check.rs:1645, check.rs:1939, coincheck.rs:264 — no DEFAULT_NOVEL_WORDS constant despite lib.rs defining DEFAULT_OFFICIAL/DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE etc.
findings.fix: Add crate::DEFAULT_NOVEL_WORDS to lib.rs; extract one NovelWordRow write/parse pair (column names owned once) used by the site writer, build_index, and the shadow-canary test; have the export pass its in-memory rows to build_index instead of round-tripping through disk.

findings.summary: FormRecord has no lemma_key field, so the folded lemma is recomputed per sink-add and per record in every consumer loop (~5% of index build)
findings.evidence: CONFIRMED deferred item. FormRecord struct (forms.rs:340-360) lacks lemma_key; RecordSink::add refolds per cell at forms.rs:415 `self.map.entry((key.clone(), form_key(lemma), entry_id))`; enrich_animate_accusatives refolds per record at forms.rs:726 `masc_animate_keys.contains(&form_key(&r.lemma))`; check.rs refolds in hot loops at :482/:498 `forms::form_key(&r.lemma) == row.lemma_key`, :1062 `official.iter().map(|r| forms::form_key(&r.lemma))`, :1222 `index.verb_valence.get(&forms::form_key(&r.lemma))` (token_grammar, per token per record), :1238 `index.noun_gender.get(&forms::form_key(&r.lemma))`.
findings.fix: Add lemma_key: String to FormRecord, computed once in RecordSink::add (the fold is already done there for the dedup key — keep it); replace every form_key(&r.lemma) call site with r.lemma_key. Record JSON serialization unchanged, so export stays byte-identical.

findings.summary: run_refresh reads and CSV-parses both dictionary files twice — official::load first, then raw_rows re-reads and re-runs read_csv_records on the same files
findings.evidence: CONFIRMED deferred item. release.rs:197-198 `let new_entries = crate::official::load(input)?; let old_entries = crate::official::load(official)?;` (official::load does read_to_string + read_csv_records, official.rs:234-244); then raw_rows at :207-210 `let text = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?; ... crate::official::read_csv_records(&text)` is called for both files at :225-226. new_entries/old_entries are used ONLY for `.len()` in the changelog entry (release.rs:253-255).
findings.fix: Read each file once; run read_csv_records once per file and derive both the row map and the entry count from that single pass (validate loadability via official's record-to-entry step on the shared records, or keep load but feed it the already-read text). Changelog output byte-identical.

findings.summary: Three JSON string escapers: forms.rs json_str (hand-rolled), check.rs json_escape (serde_json wrapper), site/layout.rs json_str (lossy variant) — the first two produce identical bytes
findings.evidence: CONFIRMED deferred item. forms.rs:1093-1111 hand-rolled `fn json_str` escaping ",\\,\n,\r,\t and <0x20 as \u{:04x} — exactly serde_json's escaping; check.rs:1495-1497 `fn json_escape(s) { serde_json::to_string(s).unwrap_or_else(|_| "\"\"".into()) }`; site/layout.rs:65-79 `pub(super) fn json_str` DIVERGES (\t → space, other control chars silently dropped).
findings.fix: Make forms::json_str pub(crate) and use it from check.rs (byte-identical to the serde path for all inputs). layout.rs's variant has different semantics — keep it but rename (e.g. json_str_lossy) with a comment, or prove callers never pass control chars and merge later; do not merge it in the frozen pass.

findings.summary: Index.noun_animate is built and stored but never read anywhere, and its doc comment falsely claims the valence check reads it
findings.evidence: Repo-wide grep for noun_animate: only check.rs:60 (field decl), :85 (built), :239 (moved into Index), :2358 (test constructor). No `.noun_animate` read exists. Doc comment at :57-59 says "the valence check reads animacy from here instead of from the paradigm shape" — the valence check (agreement_pass :1333-1366) actually reads object-shaped analyses and index.verb_valence, never noun_animate. The animacy data reaches consumers only via masc_animate_from_maps on the LOCAL maps before Index construction (:224).
findings.fix: Delete the field (and the test constructor's line) and fix the doc comment; keep noun_trait_maps returning the pair for masc_animate_from_maps. No serialized artifact touches it — provably export-neutral.

findings.summary: agent_guide() is a ~368-line format! raw string inside forms.rs documenting the entire product surface (English API, coin-check, project lexicons, notes) — wrong home, wrong medium
findings.evidence: CONFIRMED deferred item. forms.rs:1332-1699: `pub fn agent_guide() -> String { format!(r#"# Slovowiki lexical API — agent guide ..."#) }` covering `## Coinage validation (coin-check)` (:1557), `## Project lexicons (check-text --lexicon)` (:1586), `## English → Interslavic lookup` (:1421) — none owned by forms.rs. Sole caller: site/mod.rs:2042 `&crate::forms::agent_guide()`. Interpolations are just SCHEMA_VERSION, LICENSE, SHARDS; the literal needs `{{`-escaping throughout (e.g. :1622-1626), a maintenance trap.
findings.fix: Move to the site module (the only caller) as src/site/agent_guide.rs, or better an include_str! template with the three placeholders substituted — assert byte-equality with the old output in a test during the move.

findings.summary: tokenize() is a copy-paste of tokenize_with_breaks() minus break tracking — 20 duplicated lines of the same char/hyphen state machine
findings.evidence: check.rs:827-847 tokenize and :852-882 tokenize_with_breaks share identical alphabetic/hyphen-peek logic (`c == '-' && !cur.is_empty() && chars.peek().map(|n| n.is_alphabetic()).unwrap_or(false)` appears verbatim in both, :834-837 and :861-864); the with-breaks version only adds `pending_break` bookkeeping. Token output is provably identical for all inputs.
findings.fix: Survivor: tokenize_with_breaks. Reimplement `pub fn tokenize(text) { tokenize_with_breaks(text).0 }`.

findings.summary: 18 duplicated test preambles (official::load(DEFAULT_OFFICIAL).expect + build_index) across check.rs and coincheck.rs tests
findings.evidence: CONFIRMED deferred item (area share of the ~13): `grep -c "official::load(Path::new(crate::DEFAULT_OFFICIAL))"` → check.rs: 16 (e.g. :2085, :2152, :2245, :2298, :2325, :2416, :2503, :2552, :2602, :2615, :2654, :2731, :2755, :2857, :2905), coincheck.rs: 2 (:589, :745). Most immediately follow with `build_index(&entries, None, Default::default())`. check.rs already has the pattern-proving helper sample_index() at :2069.
findings.fix: Two #[cfg(test)] helpers beside sample_index(): fn official_entries() -> Vec<OfficialEntry> and fn full_index() -> Index; also consider OnceLock-caching the CSV load to cut test wall time.

findings.summary: Dead no-ops left in lib paths: `let _ = json_escape("");` in run(), and agreement_pass's index parameter discarded with `let _ = index;`
findings.evidence: check.rs:1770 `let _ = json_escape("");` immediately before `fail_gate_if_needed(summary)` — json_escape is genuinely used in write_web_suggestions (:1455,:1470), so this call suppresses nothing and does nothing. check.rs:1276-1282: `fn agreement_pass(index: &Index, ...)` then first statement `let _ = index;` — the parameter is entirely unused (prep government arrives pre-resolved in TokenGrammar.prep at :1247).
findings.fix: Delete the no-op line; drop the index parameter from agreement_pass and its two call-shape (one call site, :1040).

findings.summary: token_grammar's valence fold uses two mutable variables (char + seen-bool) where an Option<char> fold states the all-lemmas-agree rule directly
findings.evidence: CONFIRMED deferred item. check.rs:1206-1207 `let mut valence = ' '; let mut valence_lemmas_seen = false;` and :1225-1230 `if !valence_lemmas_seen { valence = v; valence_lemmas_seen = true; } else if valence != v { valence = ' '; }`.
findings.fix: Fold into `let mut valence: Option<char> = None;` with `valence = match valence { None => Some(v), Some(x) if x == v => Some(v), _ => Some(' ') }`, unwrap_or(' ') at struct build — identical truth table, one variable.

findings.summary: The genitive-of-negation guard is a triple-negated inline expression buried in the valence condition chain
findings.evidence: CONFIRMED deferred item. check.rs:1340-1344: `if a.pure_verb && a.valence == 'i' && b.pure_noun && !b.noun.is_empty() && !(i > 0 && grammar[i - 1].negation && !breaks.get(i).copied().unwrap_or(false))` — negation of (negation-token AND NOT break), inside an already 5-clause &&-chain.
findings.fix: Extract `let negated_context = i > 0 && grammar[i-1].negation && !breaks.get(i).copied().unwrap_or(false);` (or a named closure preceded_by_negation(i)) and write `&& !negated_context` — same boolean, readable at the altitude the comment (:1339 "A preceding ne abstains") already speaks.

findings.summary: Declinability probing uses raw std::panic::catch_unwind at two call sites instead of one fallible wrapper in forms.rs — panic-as-control-flow leaks stderr noise wherever the CLI quiet hook isn't installed
findings.evidence: check.rs:462-465 `std::panic::catch_unwind(std::panic::AssertUnwindSafe(|| forms::noun_paradigm_forms_with_animacy(...))).is_ok()` in validate_lexicon_row (a lib path, also reached by coin-check --lexicon-row); coincheck.rs:309-312 `std::panic::catch_unwind(std::panic::AssertUnwindSafe(|| interslavic::noun_forms(word))).ok()`. forms.rs already owns the pattern (catch() :128-130, verb_cells :290) and the quiet hook (:39-52) is only installed by main.rs command arms (:362,:411,:448,:476) — library/test callers of validate_lexicon_row print raw panic backtraces for undeclinable nouns.
findings.fix: Add forms::try_noun_paradigm_forms_with_animacy / try_noun_forms returning Option, doing the catch_unwind in one place next to catch(); both call sites become .is_some()/.map(). Identical results; centralizes the AssertUnwindSafe justification.

findings.summary: coin-check builds collision rows as serde_json::Value then string-extracts them back for the human report (`c["lemma"].as_str().unwrap_or("")`) — a stringly-typed round-trip of its own data
findings.evidence: coincheck.rs:267-289 builds `Vec<serde_json::Value>` via json!({"lemma": .., "pos": .., "status": .., "as": .., "gloss": ..}); the human path then reads it back at :477-491: `c["lemma"].as_str().unwrap_or(""), c["status"].as_str().unwrap_or(""), c["pos"].as_str().unwrap_or(""), c["as"].as_str().unwrap_or(""), c["gloss"].as_str().unwrap_or("")` — five silent-empty fallbacks for fields the same function just set.
findings.fix: A plain `struct Collision { lemma, pos, status, as_, gloss }` with #[derive(Serialize)]; JSON arm serializes it, human arm reads fields directly. Same bytes both ways, no unwrap_or masking.

stale_candidates: check.rs:891 pub fn check_tokens — no callers outside check.rs (grep -l "check_tokens(" hits only src/check.rs; used by run_eval and in-file tests); could be pub(crate), but external/doc consumers of the library API can't be ruled out
check.rs:279 pub const LEXICON_COLUMNS — referenced only inside check.rs's own error message (:323); its pub-ness suggests an external consumer that no longer exists (possibly docs)

release.rs:166 run_manifest hardcodes "data/refresh-changelog.md" while run_refresh takes changelog as a parameter — asymmetric parameterization suggests the param is only exercised by tests; either both should take it or neither

check.rs:185-192 the novel-words pos match arm maps every unknown string to "other" silently — possibly a leftover permissiveness from before the writer (site/mod.rs) constrained the vocabulary; couldn't prove any "other" row exists in committed data

forms.rs:192-194 pub fn noun_cell — thin wrapper over noun_cell_g(.., None); callers outside tests appear to be site tables and one check.rs test (:2121); if site consistently has gender available, the None wrapper may be a trap kept only for tests

audits.dataflow:
In: data/official-isv.csv (official::load), data/slavic-lemmas.cache.json (LemmaCorpus — its EXISTENCE selects export_corpus vs the fallback export in main.rs:365-371), optional caches (proto-slavic, raw-slavic-lemmas + coverage tally, wiktionary-enrich), two calibrators (data/score-calibration.json pipeline-domain, data/corpus-calibration.json banded-domain), curation notes, git metadata (BuildMeta shells out to git).
Out under out_dir: entry/*.html (3 page classes: generated / official-only / raw), sharded search index + browse/spotlight (search.rs), wiki indexes (navigation::write_wiki_indexes: all-pages, categories, graph, stats...), special pages (index, search, forms, text-check, about, metrics, datasets, proposals, deriv/, proto/), wiktionary.css, .nojekyll, novel-words.tsv, api/ (forms shards via forms::write_api, en/ shards via english_api, notes/ shards, aspect-pairs.json, suggest/ shards via check::write_web_suggestions).
Out into the REPO: data/novel-words.tsv (mod.rs:1622, relative path) — re-read in the same run by check::build_index (mod.rs:2028) and by the separate check-text/coincheck commands.
Modules touched: corpus, generator, consensus, official, calibrate, dump, enrich, thesaurus, aspect, derive, forms, check, falsefriends, flavorize, glossxref, lang, orthography, normalize; release.rs reads site::PROBE_BASELINE back.
Circular concern: check.rs imports crate::site::english_gloss_tokens (english_api.rs:329) while site::export_corpus calls check::build_index / write_web_suggestions / masc_animate_lemma_keys.
Fallback export (mod.rs:73-285) emits entry pages + search shards + 5 shell pages but NO api/ artifacts at all.

findings.summary: export_corpus is a 1,808-line god function (lines 314-2121) with ~13 distinct responsibilities and three local struct definitions (Prepared, OfficialMatch, OfficialOnlyRecord at 373-404) declared inside the function body.
findings.evidence: fn starts at 314, Ok(()) at 2120. Responsibilities counted in-code: (1) cache/calibrator loading 315-350+473-487; (2) generate pass building Prepared 405-464; (3) official-match dedup 500-536; (4) same-concept suppression 561-610; (5) id allocation 613-615; (6) families/thesaurus/xref/headword index 646-724; (7) raw-page planning + false-friends 734-767; (8) metas + aspect linking + invariant ensure! 771-950; (9) wiki indexes 1046-1057; (10) THREE render loops with search-row emission 1072-1519; (11) proposals + TSV 1537-1627; (12) forms/lemma API sinks, derivatives, raw-intl, notes shards, english API, aspect-pairs, checker suggest index 1635-2052; (13) special pages 2058-2113.
findings.fix: Split into staged functions passing explicit structs: plan_corpus_site() (pure: Prepared+metas+ids+raw plan), render_pages() (the three loops), export_api() (sinks/shards), write_shell_pages(). Pure code motion — byte-identical output, and each stage becomes unit-testable.

findings.summary: The 14-element positional search-row array is hand-format!-ed in FOUR separate loops (fallback export, generated, official-only, raw), held in sync only by a comment.
findings.evidence: mod.rs 201-213 (fallback: format!("[{},{},{},...") with hardcoded "1,1,0"), 1216-1231 (generated), 1324-1339 (official-only), 1473-1488 (raw). The comment at 1198-1212 admits it: "one 14-element positional array per entry, emitted identically by THREE loops (generated / official-only / raw) ... Keep all five sides in lock-step".
findings.fix: Survivor: a single SearchRow::build(fields: SearchRowFields) constructor in site/search.rs next to the SearchRow struct and SEARCH_JS reader; all four call sites feed it the same values. Same format string centralized → provably byte-identical.

findings.summary: site<->check circular concern: english_gloss_tokens lives in site::english_api but serves check.rs, while site::export_corpus orchestrates check's artifacts (build_index, write_web_suggestions, masc_animate_lemma_keys).
findings.evidence: english_api.rs:329 `pub fn english_gloss_tokens(...)`, re-exported at mod.rs:2134-2137; consumed by check.rs:396, 530, 1067 (`crate::site::english_gloss_tokens`). Opposite direction: mod.rs:1889-1891 `crate::check::masc_animate_lemma_keys`, 2027-2032 `crate::check::build_index(...)` + `crate::check::write_web_suggestions(out_dir, &checker_index)`.
findings.fix: Extract english_gloss_tokens plus its gloss_keys/usable_token_key dependencies into a neutral crate::gloss module (keep the site re-export one release for compat); have export_corpus call a check-owned `check::export_web_artifacts(out_dir, ...)` so the checker owns its own api/suggest surface. Breaks the cycle without touching bytes.

findings.summary: The fallback site::export mode (no lemma cache) is dead in practice AND broken when reached: it writes forms.html/text-check.html whose JS requires api/ artifacts the function never writes, and every page's nav links to a dozen pages it never generates.
findings.evidence: main.rs:365-371 dispatches export() only `if !lemmas.exists()`, but data/slavic-lemmas.cache.json is committed (git ls-files data/ lists it), so a repo checkout never takes this path. When taken: export() at mod.rs 73-285 writes forms.html (257) and text-check.html (258) but contains zero api/ writes; forms_js's routerSelftest (assets.rs:33) fetches api/router-selftest.json → fails → "Iskanje je zaprěno". layout.rs page shell (lines 24, 30-43) links special.html, all-pages.html, categories.html, site-stats.html, indices.html, portals.html, graph.html, borrowings.html, needs-review.html, contribute.html, metrics.html — none written by export().
findings.fix: Behavior-frozen-safe move: quarantine export() and its private renderer cluster (entries.rs entry_page 1396+ / status_banner / etymology_block / alternatives_block / evidence_block, search.rs home_page, special.rs about_page) into a site::fallback submodule and record the decision needed: either delete the mode (make missing-cache a hard error — behavior change requiring sign-off) or make it write the api/ artifacts it links.

findings.summary: export_corpus writes data/novel-words.tsv into the REPO via a cwd-relative path, then 400 lines later re-reads the same file from disk for check::build_index — data it already holds in memory — and three other commands consume the same hardcoded path.
findings.evidence: mod.rs:1622 `std::fs::write("data/novel-words.tsv", &tsv)?` (relative — running the binary from another cwd creates a stray ./data). mod.rs:2027-2031 `crate::check::build_index(&official_entries, Some(std::path::Path::new("data/novel-words.tsv")), ...)` re-parses what line 1622 just wrote from the in-scope `proposals` vec. Same literal path in check.rs:1645, 1939 and coincheck.rs:264, 748 — an artifact regenerated by `export` and owned by no single stage.
findings.fix: Pass the in-memory proposal rows to build_index (prove the TSV round-trip is lossless with a one-off diff test), keep the data/ write as an explicitly named 'commit artifact' step anchored to a repo-root constant (e.g. crate::NOVEL_WORDS_PATH) shared by check/coincheck instead of four string literals.

findings.summary: ~40 KB of minified client JS lives in Rust string constants with hand-rolled __PLACEHOLDER__ string-replace templating; TEXT_CHECK_JS is a single ~4.6 KB source line — unlintable, untestable except via runtime self-test fetches.
findings.evidence: assets.rs:29-50 forms_js JS blob with `__SHARDS__`/`__SUGGEST_SHARDS__`/`__FOLD_MAP__` patched via .replace() (56-62); assets.rs:70 TEXT_CHECK_JS is one line; search.rs:604-724 SEARCH_JS is ~10 KB with `__SEARCH_FOLD__` injection (search.rs:473). assets.rs itself is 30,211 bytes, mostly payload. Precedent already exists: BASE_CSS uses include_str! (assets.rs:72).
findings.fix: Move each blob verbatim to static/*.js and load with include_str! (byte-identical by construction, like BASE_CSS); keep the placeholder substitution but name the contract in one inject() helper. Enables eslint/prettier-check in CI on real .js files.

findings.summary: Renderers unwrap/index on cross-function invariants: candidates.first().unwrap() twice in entries.rs, HashMap bracket-indexing and .expect() on meta lookups in mod.rs.
findings.evidence: entries.rs:239 `let top = g.candidates.first().unwrap();` (corpus_entry_page) and entries.rs:1403 same in entry_page — the emptiness check lives in the distant caller (mod.rs:130 / the form.is_empty() skip at 410). mod.rs:899 `metas[meta_pos[&id]].aspect = ...`, mod.rs:913 `let (im, pm) = (meta_pos[&ii], meta_pos[&pi]);` — double bracket-indexing that panics if the metas/prepared invariant ever drifts. mod.rs:1106 and 1269 `.expect("generated entry meta")`.
findings.fix: Make the invariant structural: put the top candidate (and the resolved &SiteEntryMeta) into CorpusEntryInput / the render call instead of re-deriving them inside the renderer; replace meta_pos bracket-indexing with the same `let Some(..) else { anyhow::bail!/ensure! }` posture the surrounding code already uses (lines 932-948).

findings.summary: The fold+ascii_skeleton search-key builder (filter: >=2 chars, != lowercased display, dedupe) is written three times: add_official_byform_keys, the official-only loop, and the raw loop.
findings.evidence: mod.rs:50-70 add_official_byform_keys: `for k in [lower.clone(), to_standard(&lower), ascii_skeleton(byform)] { if k.chars().count() >= 2 && k != display_lower && !keys.iter().any(...) }`; mod.rs:1291-1299 official-only loop: `for k in [fold.clone(), ascii_skeleton(isv)] { if k.chars().count() >= 2 && k != isv.to_lowercase() && ... }`; mod.rs:1447-1459 raw loop: identical shape over `[to_standard(&disp_lower), ascii_skeleton(&display)]`.
findings.fix: Survivor: one `push_fold_keys(keys, variants, display_lower, rank)` helper in site/search.rs (it already owns search_keys); add_official_byform_keys becomes a thin wrapper. Same iteration order → identical key lists.

findings.summary: The sameInLanguages razumlivost "null-or-rounded-percent" block is duplicated verbatim between the fallback export and the official-only loop, beside a model.rs helper (razum_pct) that covers only the non-null half.
findings.evidence: mod.rs:191-198 and mod.rs:1313-1320 are token-identical: `let same_in = e.same_in_langs(); if same_in.is_empty() { "null".to_string() } else { (crate::lang::razumlivost(&same_in).overall.round() as u32).to_string() }`. model.rs:297-300 razum_pct already wraps the round-as-u32 half for &[String].
findings.fix: Survivor: `razum_same_in_cell(&OfficialEntry) -> String` in site/model.rs next to razum_pct; both call sites delegate. Byte-identical output.

findings.summary: The shared shell-write tail (write_search_index, remove_file(search.json), wiktionary.css, .nojekyll, plus the index/search/forms/text-check/about page writes) is duplicated between export and export_corpus.
findings.evidence: mod.rs:231-234 vs 1521-1530: both do write_search_index → `let _ = std::fs::remove_file(out_dir.join("search.json"));` → `std::fs::write(out_dir.join("wiktionary.css"), css())` → `.nojekyll`. mod.rs:255-258 vs 2076-2079: identical `search.html`/`forms.html`/`text-check.html` writes (search_page(), forms_page(), text_check_page()).
findings.fix: Survivor: a `write_common_shell(out_dir, search_rows) -> Result<(usize,usize)>` helper in site/mod.rs called by both exports (also shrinks the fallback so its eventual removal is smaller).

findings.summary: `covered` is declared 324 lines before first use and the first operation on it is .clear() of an always-empty set — leftover from a removed first-pass population.
findings.evidence: mod.rs:369 `let mut covered: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();` with a comment about "surviving generated pages"; grep shows no insert between 369 and mod.rs:693 `covered.clear();` — the only inserts are at 696 and 708, after the clear. The comment at 690-692 ("The earlier candidate-coverage set includes top-5 alternatives...") describes code that no longer exists.
findings.fix: Declare `covered` at line 693, delete the clear() and the stale comment at 369-370. No behavior change.

findings.summary: json_str is a hand-rolled JSON string encoder that silently drops all control chars < 0x20 and rewrites tab to space, living beside serde_json which export_corpus already uses for the notes shards.
findings.evidence: layout.rs:65-79: `'\t' => out.push(' '), c if (c as u32) < 0x20 => {}` — lossy, undocumented at call sites; it encodes every search-row field and the aspect-pairs JSON (mod.rs:2008-2022 builds JSON by write!). serde_json is used ~80 lines away in the same function (mod.rs:1916-1941).
findings.fix: Frozen-safe move: keep the encoder (swapping to serde_json would change bytes for tab/control inputs) but rename to lossy_json_str, document the two lossy rules, and pin them with unit tests; schedule the serde swap for a declared byte-thaw.

findings.summary: Two parallel English gloss tokenizers with silently different semantics: dump::gloss_tokens (used by export_corpus dedup/suppression/search keys) vs site::english_api::english_gloss_tokens (used by the checker's overlap test).
findings.evidence: dump.rs:1685-1696: lowercase, split on non-alphabetic, len>=3, 25-word stoplist, returns Vec. english_api.rs:329-340: routes through gloss_keys + usable_token_key, returns BTreeSet, "the SAME key extraction and stopword discipline the English index build uses". export_corpus uses the dump one at mod.rs:503-504, 565, 1172; check.rs uses the site one — two overlap definitions for the same concept in one pipeline.
findings.fix: Co-locate both in the proposed crate::gloss module with contract-stating names (e.g. dedup_gloss_tokens vs english_index_tokens) and a doc comment explaining why they differ; do NOT merge semantics under the freeze — that would change suppression and search output.

findings.summary: proto_stem re-allocates and re-sorts its constant SUFFIXES table on every call, and the table is compile-time sortable.
findings.evidence: model.rs:337-338 inside fn proto_stem: `let mut suffixes = SUFFIXES.to_vec(); suffixes.sort_by_key(|suffix| std::cmp::Reverse(suffix.chars().count()));` — SUFFIXES is a `const &[&str]` (333-336); the function runs once per prepared entry via family_key (mod.rs:652).
findings.fix: Order the const table longest-first (assert the ordering in a unit test) or sort once in a LazyLock; identical stripping result, removes a per-entry alloc+sort in the export hot loop.

stale_candidates: src/site/entries.rs:1396-1833 fallback-only renderer cluster (entry_page, status_banner, etymology_block, alternatives_block, evidence_block, calibration_note) — reachable only via site::export, which never runs on a checkout that has the committed data/slavic-lemmas.cache.json
src/site/search.rs home_page and src/site/special.rs about_page — same fallback-only reachability (corpus path uses corpus_home/corpus_about instead)

src/site/assets.rs EXTRA_CSS lines ~199-208 (a.chip/entry-tabs pill styling) — fully negated by the later '!important' reset layer at lines 250-253 ('Strict wiki link styling'); dead CSS shipped on every page, but removal changes export bytes so it needs a declared byte-thaw

src/site/mod.rs:232 and :1528 remove_file("search.json") — migration hygiene for the retired monolithic index (retired per comment at search.rs:160); removable once no deployment predates the sharded index

src/site/mod.rs:50 add_official_byform_keys' third variant key `lower.clone()` — on the fallback path display==byform for most rows so the `k != display_lower` guard usually rejects it; suspect only the folded/skeleton variants ever survive (not proven across byform lists)

fallback export's hardcoded search-row elements "1,1,0" and empty quality/ancestor cells (mod.rs:202-213) — schema padding that exists only to keep SEARCH_JS parsing one shape; dies with the fallback mode

audits.area: site-api (src/site: english_api.rs, search.rs, special.rs, navigation.rs, coverage.rs, tests.rs)
audits.dataflow:
Inputs: official dictionary entries, corpus cognate sets (crate::corpus/dump caches), FormRecord streams (crate::forms), falsefriends notes, aspect meta, calibrators, proto cache, curation-notes.json.
Outputs: english_api.rs writes site/api/en/{0..255}.json + selftest.json + meta.json; search.rs writes search/{shard}.json + manifest/browse/spotlight; navigation.rs writes ~15 wiki index page families plus entries.json/edges.json/categories.json/roots.json; special.rs writes rule/, proto/, deriv/ pages, datasets.html, metrics.html, proposals; coverage.rs writes target/eval/raw-coverage.{md,json} via the `coverage` CLI.
coverage.rs is NOT reporting-only: mod.rs::export_corpus imports 9 of its functions (inject_generated_derivatives feeds the forms/lemma sinks; plan_raw_pages, raw_intl_candidates/probabilities, select_official_surface, official_surface_maps drive the export itself). Its run_coverage additionally REPLICATES the export dedup pipeline to reconcile counts.
navigation.rs is a grab-bag: entry-meta/category construction (incl. a ~70-line hardcoded topic taxonomy), graph edge/backlink building, ~20 HTML page renderers, the write_wiki_indexes IO orchestrator, hand-rolled JSON dataset emitters, entry-page fragments (infobox/tabs/blocks/razum rows) consumed by entries.rs, and inflection-issue detection that calls crate::forms under catch_unwind.
english_api.rs mixes three programs: shard/index BUILD (build_english_index/write_en_api, called from export_corpus), a CLI lookup runtime that reads back the exported JSON (en_ladder/run_en_lookup/run_en_batch, dispatched from main.rs), and the tracked translation-probe runner writing target/eval/translation-probe.md (PROBE_BASELINE also read by release.rs).
Cross-module touch points: check.rs calls crate::site::english_gloss_tokens (3 sites); coverage.rs:1418 produces the "label ← base (gloss…)" derivative-gloss format that english_api.rs string-parses; both mod.rs::export (legacy) and export_corpus write index/search/forms/text-check/about pages.

findings.summary: build_corpus_render_index re-implements ~170 lines of export_corpus's match/homograph-dedup/same-concept-suppression/official-only-id pipeline, kept synchronized only by comment discipline
findings.evidence: coverage.rs:56 'Minimal replica of export_corpus's per-set state… Kept in lock-step with export_corpus.' Lines 250-284 (homograph dedup with rank = gloss-token overlap + score*1000) and 286-321 (same-concept suppression) are near-verbatim copies of mod.rs:500-536 and mod.rs:562-594 (same rank closures, same by_form fold, same kept-token test). Drift is only caught at runtime: run_coverage line 1281 'anyhow::bail!("coverage reconciliation FAILED")'.
findings.fix: Extract one prepare-pipeline (generate → match → homograph dedup → suppression → id allocation) into a shared function consumed by both export_corpus and run_coverage; CovPrepared/Prepared unify. Byte-identity provable because run_coverage already asserts its replica reconciles with export counts.

findings.summary: english_gloss_tokens (plus gloss_keys and the stopword lists it rides on) lives in site::english_api but exists to serve check.rs, which reaches across the module boundary at three sites
findings.evidence: english_api.rs:329 'pub fn english_gloss_tokens… This is the overlap test behind check-text's project-lexicon consistency warning (V13 item 1)'. check.rs:396 'gloss_tokens: crate::site::english_gloss_tokens(gloss)', also check.rs:530 and check.rs:1067. mod.rs:2134-2136 pub-exports it solely for this.
findings.fix: Move English gloss tokenization (gloss_keys, HEAD_STOPWORDS/TOKEN_STOPWORDS, english_gloss_tokens, normalize_english_query) to a neutral module (e.g. a new src/english.rs beside dump::gloss_tokens/falsefriends::gloss_tokens), imported by both site build and check.

findings.summary: english_api.rs (1896 lines) fuses three separately-owned programs: shard index BUILD, CLI lookup runtime over the exported JSON, and the translation-probe eval-report writer
findings.evidence: Build half: build_english_index/write_en_api (1075-1357) called from mod.rs:1976. CLI half begins at the banner comment line 498-505 ('`en` CLI… reads the exporter's OWN emitted artifacts') — en_ladder/run_en_lookup/run_en_batch dispatched from main.rs:430-431. Probe half: run_translation_probe (860-990) writes target/eval/translation-probe.md; PROBE_BASELINE (850) is read by release.rs:134.
findings.fix: Split into english_api/build.rs (index construction + write_en_api), english_api/cli.rs (ladder/lookup/batch), and move the probe runner + PROBE_BASELINE/PROBE_FILE next to the other eval reporters (eval.rs owns target/eval artifacts elsewhere). Pure code motion; export bytes untouched.

findings.summary: The derivative gloss format "label ← base (gloss…)" is produced in coverage.rs and string-parsed back in english_api.rs — an undeclared cross-module text schema including a 50-char truncation both sides must agree on
findings.evidence: coverage.rs:1418 'let gloss = format!("{} ← {} ({})", d.label, base, truncate(base_gloss, 50));'. english_api.rs:196-205 english_lookup_gloss keys off 'gloss.contains('←')' and extracts the parenthetical; gloss_keys:183-186 skips '…'-bearing segments only when derived. english_api.rs test comment 1727: 'coverage.rs truncates the base gloss at 50 chars with a trailing `…`; the cut fragment must not become an English key.'
findings.fix: Define the derivative-gloss constructor and parser as a paired API in one module (format_deriv_gloss / parse_deriv_gloss with the 50-char constant), used by inject_generated_derivatives and english_lookup_gloss; or carry base-gloss provenance structurally on FormRecord instead of re-parsing the display string.

findings.summary: Two export paths (legacy `export` and `export_corpus`) both write index.html, search.html, forms.html, text-check.html and an about/metrics page — the same artifacts regenerated by two divergent code paths behind one CLI subcommand
findings.evidence: mod.rs:255-266 (export writes index/search/forms/text-check/about) and mod.rs:2076-2089 (export_corpus writes the same four plus metrics_page). main.rs:367-371 picks between them by whether DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE exists: 'if lemmas.exists() { site::export_corpus(...) } else { site::export(...) }'.
findings.fix: Extract the shared static-page block into one writer used by both; then evaluate retiring the ~240-line legacy export() fallback entirely (it renders a different site from the same output dir, so its pages are dead the moment the cache exists).

findings.summary: navigation.rs (2123 lines) mixes six concerns: meta/category model construction, a hardcoded topic taxonomy, graph edge building, ~20 page renderers, the write_wiki_indexes IO orchestrator, JSON dataset emitters, and entry-page fragments consumed by entries.rs
findings.evidence: entry_meta/entry_categories/branch_pattern (29-186), topic_category_path 70-line if-chain (260-327), build_edges/backlinks_by_target (460-540), write_wiki_indexes writing ~15 file families (1292-1536), entries_json/graph_json/categories_json/roots_json (1939-2122), and entry-page fragments entry_tabs/entry_infobox/entry_wiki_blocks/razum_row (1559-1761) which mod.rs imports for entry rendering, not navigation.
findings.fix: Split along data flow: categories.rs (taxonomy + tree + category pages), graph.rs (edges/backlinks/graph page+json), move entry fragments (infobox/tabs/wiki blocks/razum/raw_credit_line) into entries.rs which is their only consumer context, and gather the hand-rolled *_json emitters into one datasets module. Pure code motion.

findings.summary: Four byte-identical private `fn page` wrappers around layout::page + search_js() in search.rs, navigation.rs, special.rs, entries.rs
findings.evidence: grep: 'super::layout::page(title, body, depth, search_js())' at entries.rs:27, search.rs:15, navigation.rs:26, special.rs:20 — each preceded by the same three-line fn definition.
findings.fix: Survivor: one pub(super) layout::std_page(title, body, depth) in layout.rs that injects search_js() itself; delete the four wrappers.

findings.summary: add_official_byform_keys (mod.rs) duplicates search.rs::search_keys' fold-triple logic: [lowercase, to_standard(lower), ascii_skeleton] with len>=2 gate and dedup
findings.evidence: mod.rs:58-66 'for k in [lower.clone(), crate::orthography::to_standard(&lower), crate::orthography::ascii_skeleton(byform)] { if k.chars().count() >= 2 && k != display_lower && !keys.iter().any(...)' vs search.rs:488-496 'for k in [lower.clone(), crate::orthography::to_standard(&lower), crate::orthography::ascii_skeleton(&c.form)] { if k.chars().count() >= 2 && seen.insert(...)'; collect_source_aliases (search.rs:565-574) is a third variant over [latin, to_standard(latin), skeleton].
findings.fix: Survivor: a fold_variants(s) -> [String;3] helper in search.rs (or orthography), consumed by search_keys, add_official_byform_keys and collect_source_aliases; move add_official_byform_keys into search.rs beside its twin.

findings.summary: load_curation_notes swallows both read AND parse errors — a malformed data/curation-notes.json silently drops every curation note site-wide
findings.evidence: navigation.rs:424-430: 'let Ok(raw) = std::fs::read_to_string(path) else { return HashMap::new(); }; serde_json::from_str::<HashMap<String,String>>(&raw).unwrap_or_default()' — a JSON typo yields an empty map with no diagnostic.
