Same pattern in coverage.rs:1380 'serde_json::to_vec_pretty(&report).unwrap_or_default()' would write an empty raw-coverage.json on serialize failure.
findings.fix: Missing file stays a silent default (legitimate optional input), but a present-yet-unparsable file should return Err (or at minimum print a loud warning). Byte-identical on all valid inputs; only the currently-silent corruption path changes.

findings.summary: Six hand-rolled JSON emitters (entries_json, graph_json, categories_json, roots_json, rules_json, build_json) build JSON by string concatenation beside serde_json used in the same files
findings.evidence: navigation.rs:2024-2067 entries_json manually assembles '{"id":…,"title":…}' with json_str and "null" strings; graph_json 1939-1956, categories_json 2069-2105, roots_json 2107-2122; special.rs:930-938 build_json, 327-345 rules_json — while write_search_index (search.rs:425-436) and write_en_api use serde_json::json! for equivalent output in the same module family.
findings.fix: Unify behind serde_json::json! value construction with a byte-diff verification on a frozen export (the emitters' formatting — pretty vs compact, \n placement — must be reproduced or verified identical); at minimum collapse the six writers onto one shared row-builder to stop the copy-paste of the null/escape idioms.

findings.summary: has_inflection_issue uses std::panic::catch_unwind around interslavic paradigm calls as control flow, and recomputes full noun/adj/verb paradigms for every entry during export
findings.evidence: navigation.rs:990 'std::panic::catch_unwind(|| crate::forms::noun_paradigm_forms(bare, None)).ok()' and :1001 'std::panic::catch_unwind(|| interslavic::adj_forms(bare)).ok()' — panic-as-Option in a lib path, with a per-cell fallback (noun_cell_g/adj_cell) that re-derives the paradigm cell-by-cell after a panic; inflection_issue(m) runs this for every noun/proper_noun/adj/verb meta in inflection_issues_page.
findings.fix: Route through the same quiet-hook/panic-counting wrapper forms.rs already owns (forms.rs has its own 2 catch_unwind sites and inflection_panic_count), exposing one non-panicking paradigm API; compute the issue flag once per entry alongside the entry's own paradigm render instead of re-deriving.

findings.summary: The en CLI half parses its own exporter's shards as untyped serde_json::Value with stringly field access, despite EnglishCandidate defining the exact schema in the same file
findings.evidence: sense_note_for (528-552) and display_order (557-568) match on c["status"], c["match"], c["lemma"] string literals; en_ladder/first_candidate (687-723) mutate Values with c["step"] = json!(…); en_selftest:594-595 hides malformed fields via 'sample[2].as_u64().unwrap_or(u64::MAX) as u32'. EnglishCandidate (69-97) is Serialize-only.
findings.fix: Derive Deserialize on EnglishCandidate (+ a small LadderCandidate wrapper for step/key) and type the CLI path; field-name typos become compile errors instead of silent None. Output text/JSON unchanged.

findings.summary: Dead statements: coverage.rs declares `covered` 130 lines before its only real use then clear()s the still-empty set; navigation.rs has a pointless by_src.clear() after last use and an unused `all` parameter
findings.evidence: coverage.rs:211 'let mut covered: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();' is never touched until :344 'covered.clear();' (nothing inserted in between). navigation.rs:817 'by_src.clear();' immediately before the value drops at end of borrowing_portal_page; navigation.rs:754 'let _ = all;' — language_portal_page's third parameter is never used (call site mod-level passes metas for nothing, nav.rs:1376).
findings.fix: Declare `covered` at line 344 and drop the clear; delete by_src.clear() and the needless mut; remove the `all` parameter from language_portal_page and its call site. Provably byte-identical.

findings.summary: metrics_page and about_page hardcode measured accuracy figures (41,65% / 49,59% / 58,31% / 20,1% / oracle deltas) as HTML literals regenerated only by hand — doc-rot by construction
findings.evidence: special.rs:992-996 '41,65%… 49,59%… 60,48% / 63,12%… 0,224' inside a r##"…"## literal; :1035 '58,31% točno / 62,84% normalizovano na 7 398 zapisah'; :1040-1041 proto-eval 20,1%/46,68%. Only the calibration section (1012-1031) renders live from the committed calibrator; everything else is frozen prose that eval reruns will silently contradict.
findings.fix: Hoist the figures into one named-constants block (with a comment tying each to its target/eval artifact) or render them from a committed metrics snapshot the eval commands update — keeping today's exact strings preserves bytes while making rot a one-file diff.

stale_candidates: site::export (mod.rs:73, ~240 lines) — the legacy official-dictionary-seeded site; only reachable when data/isv-lemmas cache is absent (main.rs:367). Likely never exercised in the documented workflow, but cannot be proven dead without confirming the cache is guaranteed present in all environments.

language_portal_page's `all: &[SiteEntryMeta]` parameter (navigation.rs:721,754) — provably unused inside the function ('let _ = all;'); listed here as an API-shape stale, fix bundled in the dead-statements finding.
borrowing_portal_page's `mut by_src` + `by_src.clear()` (navigation.rs:801,817) — dead mutation just before drop.

metrics_page/about_page hardcoded 41,65%/49,59%/58,31% figures (special.rs:992+, 1035, 1092-1101) — suspected rotted relative to current target/eval outputs; could not verify without rerunning evaluate/corpus-eval.

search.rs hosts home_page/HomeRow/strength_cell (search.rs:40-157) — not stale but misplaced relative to the module's stated charter ('Search routing, folding, sharding'); the home page is a page renderer, not search machinery.
special.rs::rule_key (special.rs:109) — single caller (rules_json); trivial indirection that could inline, kept as-is if the naming is considered documentation.

EnglishApiCounts.bytes (english_api.rs:123) — accumulated across shards+selftest+meta but check whether mod.rs prints it; only .keys/.candidates/.largest_shard confirmed used at mod.rs:1987-1991 (bytes may be reported elsewhere in the same println; unverified).

audits.area: eval (src/eval.rs, src/inflect_eval.rs, src/calibrate.rs, src/falsefriends.rs, src/pipeline.rs, src/generator.rs)
audits.dataflow:
In: data/official-isv.csv (official::load), Proto-Slavic cache (DEFAULT_PROTO_CACHE), lemma corpus (DEFAULT_LEMMA_CACHE), raw+enrich caches (falsefriends only).
Benchmarks in eval.rs, each a CLI subcommand: evaluate (run: kept ladder + rejected experiments -> target/eval/{candidate-generation-summary.json,-report.md,methodology.md,predictions.csv,regressions/improvements.csv,errors-sample.csv} AND COMMITTED data/score-calibration.json), corpus-eval (--fit writes COMMITTED data/corpus-calibration.json), audit (audit-misses.csv, stage-attribution.md), oracle (oracle-ladder.md), select-eval (cluster-selection.md), rep-eval (rep-selection.md), synonym-eval, evidence-eval, multiword-eval, proto-eval, explain (stdout), aspect-eval (aspect-pairs.{tsv,md}, frozen by FNV manifest), inflect_eval.rs (inflection-report.md).
CI-gated: evaluate (39.5% exact floor + git-diff-clean on data/score-calibration.json + target/eval/methodology.md), aspect-eval (frozen 1440-pair manifest, diff-clean on aspect-pairs.md/tsv), inflect-eval (struct-path==getter hard ensure). Diagnostic-only: audit, oracle (V7 ladder, still wired), select-eval, rep-eval, synonym-eval, evidence-eval, multiword-eval, corpus-eval headline, proto-eval, explain.
Cross-module: pipeline.rs is the shared leakage-free generate path (consensus + proto_link + proto engine) used by eval, aspect, site/coverage; generator.rs wraps it for the site (official match status) and is used by site/mod.rs and eval::explain. calibrate.rs artifacts are read by site/mod.rs (export). eval::is_holdout_id is the ONE seeded split shared with derive.rs; calibrate.rs borrows derive::wilson_lower. falsefriends.rs computes notes for site/mod.rs export and check.rs (check-text/check-eval); its gloss_tokens serves site/coverage.
Nonlinear edges: two benchmark commands (evaluate, corpus-eval --fit) write committed data/ files that export later reads; the evaluate write is buried inside write_methodology.

findings.summary: The benchmark harness preamble is copy-pasted across ~8 run_* commands: official::load + .filter(is_benchmarkable) + load_proto_index() + ConsensusConfig::production() + per-entry build_input -> skip if no modern form -> pipeline::generate -> cands.first() form -> exact/normalized match.
findings.evidence: Identical blocks at run_audit (791-835), run_oracle (965-1006), run_select_eval (1088-1200), run_synonym_eval (1316-1326), run_evidence_eval (1436-1543), run_rep_eval (2336-2371), run_proto_engine (2445-2491), evaluate_config (519-535); e.g. `let pred = cands.first().map(|c| c.form.clone()).unwrap_or_default(); ex += ortho::exact_match(&pred, &entry.isv) as usize;` appears near-verbatim 6+ times.
findings.fix: Extract a shared harness in eval.rs: `scorable_meanings(path) -> Vec<(OfficialEntry, MeaningInput)>` plus a `score(pred, entry)` helper; each run_* keeps its own loop body. Pure code motion, byte-identical reports.

findings.summary: fit_isotonic_deciles and fit_wilson_isotonic_deciles are the same PAVA pool/spread algorithm duplicated; they differ only in the per-bin statistic (raw mean vs derive::wilson_lower) and a final clamp.
findings.evidence: Lines 108-134 and 180-207 are line-for-line identical pooling loops (`while pools.len() >= 2 && pools[pools.len()-2].1 > pools[pools.len()-1].1 { ... }` and the iso spread loop); the doc at 170-172 admits: "the same recipe `evaluate` applies inline ... kept separate there to leave the benchmark path byte-stable; a future refactor can unify".
findings.fix: One private `fn pava_deciles(bins: [(usize, f64); 10]) -> [f64; 10]`; both pub fns become thin wrappers supplying mean vs wilson_lower per bin (clamp stays in the wilson wrapper). f64 ops unchanged -> identical fitted deciles.

findings.summary: write_methodology's eval_split closure reimplements calibrate::ece_brier exactly (same decile binning, ECE, Brier), and run_corpus_eval re-inlines the ECE half a third time.
findings.evidence: eval.rs 3436-3462 (`let eval_split = |rs: &[&EntryResult], use_cal: bool| -> (f64, f64)`) is the same b10/brier/ece computation as calibrate.rs 212-236 `pub fn ece_brier`; eval.rs 429-443 repeats the pooled-ECE loop (`ece_cal += (*bn as f64 / n) * (sp / *bn as f64 - ...)`).
findings.fix: Replace eval_split with `calibrate::ece_brier(&samples, use_cal.then_some(&iso))` after mapping EntryResult->(score, normalized); identical f64 arithmetic makes methodology.md byte-identical (verifiable against the CI git-diff guard).

findings.summary: write_methodology (named as a target/eval report writer) silently persists the COMMITTED calibrator data/score-calibration.json; together with corpus-eval --fit writing data/corpus-calibration.json, two benchmark commands mutate data/ artifacts that export later consumes.
findings.evidence: eval.rs:3497 `std::fs::write(crate::calibrate::PATH, serde_json::to_string_pretty(&cal)?)?;` inside `fn write_methodology`; eval.rs:465-468 writes CORPUS_CALIBRATION_PATH inside run_corpus_eval; site/mod.rs:85-92 and 474 load both at export time; ci.yml enforces `git diff --exit-code -- data/score-calibration.json target/eval/methodology.md`.
findings.fix: Hoist the calibrator fit+persist out of write_methodology into an explicitly named stage called from run() (e.g. fit_and_persist_pipeline_calibrator), and record both evaluate->data/*.json->export edges in PIPELINE.md. Pure code motion.

findings.summary: run_select_eval calls cands.first().unwrap() while an empty candidate list is a real state the sibling code defends against.
findings.evidence: Line 1207: `if winning_root_key(cands.first().unwrap()) == official_key` — guarded only by official_present, which is computed from input forms, not from cands; attribute_miss (652-654) explicitly handles `let Some(top) = cands.first() else { return ("0-no-candidate", ...) }`, proving empty cands occurs.
findings.fix: Replace with `if cands.first().is_some_and(|t| winning_root_key(t) == official_key)`; identical counts on every entry that has candidates, no panic on the empty case.

findings.summary: run_multiword_eval hand-rolls its own aspect-pair detection (gloss-keyed ipf/pf maps + greedy 1:1 consonant-root matching), parallel to aspect::detect_pairs which the CI-gated aspect-eval uses on the same corpus.
findings.evidence: eval.rs 1898-1965 builds by_gloss_ipf/by_gloss_pf from `e.pos_raw.contains("ipf.")` and matches with `ki.ends_with(&kq) || kq.ends_with(&ki) || ortho::shares_consonant_root(&ki, &kq)`; run_aspect_eval (2067) uses `crate::aspect::detect_pairs(&entries)` with a frozen 1440-pair manifest — two detectors for one concept.
findings.fix: Delegate multiword-eval's aspect slice to aspect::detect_pairs (survivor), or drop the slice and point the report at aspect-pairs.md. Numbers change only in the diagnostic-only multiword-aspect.md, never in a CI-gated artifact — call that out in the refactor note.

findings.summary: The percent closure `let pct = |a, b| if b == 0 { 0.0 } else { 100.0 * a as f32 / b as f32 }` is copy-pasted 12 times across eval.rs and inflect_eval.rs (plus a `rate` alias and two one-arg variants).
findings.evidence: Definitions at eval.rs 287, 875, 1011, 1213, 1342, 1616, 1759, 2091 (f64 variant), 2384, 2509 (`let rate = ...`), 3244 and inflect_eval.rs 272 — grep-verified identical bodies.
findings.fix: One module-level `fn pct(a: usize, b: usize) -> f32` in eval.rs (keep the single f64 variant at 2091 separate to preserve aspect-eval's frozen report bytes); mechanical substitution.

findings.summary: Three parallel gloss tokenizers with the same name family and different semantics cross module boundaries: dump::gloss_tokens (extraction cache module, used by eval/thesaurus), falsefriends::gloss_tokens (check-notes module, used by site/coverage), site::english_api::english_gloss_tokens.
findings.evidence: dump.rs:1685 (Vec<String>, len>=3, 23 stopwords, no stemming) vs falsefriends.rs:182 (BTreeSet, strip_parens, ~60 stopwords, light_stem, len>=2) vs site/english_api.rs:329; site/coverage.rs imports falsefriends::gloss_tokens at 510/520/659/678 while eval.rs uses dump::gloss_tokens at 325/343/1456 — callers must know which of three same-named tokenizers they hold.
findings.fix: Create a `gloss` text-utils module hosting all three under distinguishing names (e.g. cache_gloss_tokens, stemmed_gloss_tokens, english_gloss_tokens) with bodies moved verbatim; re-export from old paths during transition. Fixes the dump<-eval and falsefriends<-site reaches without changing any token set.

findings.summary: load_proto_index aborts via panic! inside an Option-returning helper, though all 10 callers are Result-returning commands that could propagate the error.
findings.evidence: Lines 121-122: `crate::dump::load_optional(path, crate::dump::ProtoIndex::load).unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("{}: {e:#}", path.display()))` — the comment (107-110) justifies aborting but not the mechanism; every run_* returns anyhow::Result.
findings.fix: Change signature to `Result<Option<ProtoIndex>>` and `?` at the 10 call sites; same abort-on-corrupt-cache behavior, ordinary error path instead of a lib-path panic. CLI-output-only change, export untouched.

findings.summary: run_corpus_eval hand-rolls two near-copies of OfficialEntry::is_benchmarkable that silently diverge from it (missing the '!', '"', '(', '[' and POS gates).
findings.evidence: Lines 220-225 `if input.forms.iter().filter(|f| f.modern).count() < 2 || entry.isv.trim().is_empty() { continue; } if entry.isv.contains(' ') || entry.isv.contains('#')` and line 322 repeat a weaker predicate than official.rs:69-82 `is_benchmarkable` (which also excludes '!', '"', '(', '[' and non-content POS).
findings.fix: Name the corpus-eval predicate once (e.g. `fn corpus_scorable(e) -> bool` with the exact current semantics, documented as intentionally looser than is_benchmarkable) and use it at both sites — freezes today's denominators while removing the drift hazard.

findings.summary: Generation::top() is a dead pub method — zero callers anywhere in src/ or tools/.
findings.evidence: generator.rs:25-27 `pub fn top(&self) -> Option<&Candidate> { self.candidates.first() }`; `grep -rn "\.top()" src tools` returns nothing; site/mod.rs:117 and eval.rs:2620 use .candidates/.match_status directly.
findings.fix: Delete the method (and the impl block if it becomes empty). Zero behavior change.

findings.summary: Doc rot: generate_oracle's doc says "only the `--diagnostic-oracle` eval path passes a non-None oracle", but no such CLI flag exists; the real callers are the oracle/select-eval/rep-eval subcommands.
findings.evidence: pipeline.rs:29-31 names `--diagnostic-oracle`; `grep -rn "diagnostic-oracle" src/main.rs` is empty; actual non-None oracle callers are eval.rs:1001, 1194, 2367 (run_oracle, run_select_eval, run_rep_eval via consensus::Oracle).
findings.fix: Rewrite the comment to name the three diagnostic subcommands. Comment-only.

findings.summary: CORPUS_COVERAGE_SCORE_DOMAIN ("reserved domain for a future calibrator") is superseded by CORPUS_BANDED_DOMAIN v2, which is what corpus-eval --fit actually writes; the reserved const's only remaining use is as an arbitrary wrong-domain string in one test.
findings.evidence: calibrate.rs:142-144 declares it "Reserved ... for a future calibrator"; the corpus calibrator ships `score_domain: crate::calibrate::CORPUS_BANDED_DOMAIN` (eval.rs:454); the only non-defining reference is the domain-mismatch test at calibrate.rs:319.
findings.fix: Delete the const and use a literal (e.g. "some-other-domain-v0") in the test; prevents anyone wiring the dead v1 domain into a new consumer.

findings.summary: calibrate.rs reaches into derive.rs for the statistics primitive wilson_lower — a Wilson bound has nothing to do with derivational families; it lives there only because derive used it first.
findings.evidence: calibrate.rs:113 `pools.push((*n as f64, crate::derive::wilson_lower(*hits, *n)));`; derive.rs:369 `pub(crate) fn wilson_lower(k: usize, n: usize) -> f64` is used by derive.rs:417 and calibrate.
findings.fix: Move wilson_lower into calibrate.rs (the module already owning ECE/Brier/PAVA) or a small stats module, and have derive.rs call it there. Pure move, identical f64 math.

stale_candidates: src/eval.rs run_rep_eval 'medoid' rung (line 2378): production now ships medoid_representative=true (consensus.rs:209/252), so forcing rep_rule="medoid" should reproduce the production row — the rung looks redundant post-'+medoid-rep' adoption, but I did not diff the two code paths in consensus::pick (559 vs 640) to prove identical output.

src/eval.rs pipeline.rs:114-132 cons_branch_cov: computed at 116, discarded at 132 via `let _ = cons_branch_cov;` — kept alive only to document a rejected experiment; deletable with the comment retained.
src/calibrate.rs Calibration::load (241): pub, but every external caller goes through load_for_domain (site/mod.rs:85, 2090); could be private.

src/eval.rs run_oracle V7 oracle ladder (965): wired and functional, but purely diagnostic and its headroom numbers (e.g. +3.9pp oracle-cluster) are already frozen into doc comments elsewhere (run_select_eval doc, rung descriptions) — doc rot risk if the ladder drifts; not dead, but a candidate for demotion to docs/history.
IMPROVEMENT_PROMPT.md at repo root: untracked leftover (git status '??'); prior briefs were archived under docs/history (commit c2aedf2).

src/eval.rs generator::generate (Option<&str> wrapper, generator.rs:57): single caller eval.rs:2620 (explain); site uses generate_with_official_byforms directly — thin wrapper that could be inlined at its one call site.

audits.dataflow:
Ops layer = src/main.rs (27 flat clap subcommands dispatching into lib modules), Makefile (16 targets wrapping a subset), 3 workflows, tools/ validators.
Groups: BUILD = export, extract-proto/-lemmas/-raw-slavic/-enrich, coverage; EVAL = evaluate, proto-/corpus-/derive-/multiword-/aspect-/evidence-/inflect-/checktext-eval, audit, oracle, select-/rep-/synonym-eval; AUTHOR/CHECK = check-text, coin-check, en, translation-probe, explain; RELEASE = data-manifest, refresh-official.
In: committed data/ caches (proto, lemma, raw-lemma, enrich), data/official-isv.csv, local 23GB dumps (extract-* only). Out: site/ (deployed by pages.yml on every master push), 22 TRACKED reports under gitignored target/eval/, and committed data/ artifacts written by three different commands: export -> data/novel-words.tsv (site/mod.rs:1622, hardcoded path), evaluate -> data/score-calibration.json (eval.rs:3497), corpus-eval --fit -> data/corpus-calibration.json; ci.yml freezes all three via `git diff --exit-code` (lines 49, 101).
ci.yml runs fmt/build/clippy/test + aspect-eval frozen-manifest guard + double-export determinism hash + 3 tools/ validators + inflect-eval + translation probe + data-manifest + changelog guard + accuracy floor (stdout grep). pages.yml exports and deploys; data-release.yml verifies MANIFEST on data-v* tags. Toolchain pin 1.93.1 appears in rust-toolchain.toml AND hardcoded in all 3 workflows.
xtask verdict: not warranted — the binary IS the product CLI and Makefile is thin; the behavior-frozen wins are a shared clap EvalArgs flatten and a composite setup-rust action; nested namespacing (e.g. `eval <probe>`) would change CLI strings and is out of frozen scope.
tools/__pycache__: on disk but NOT committed; .gitignore covers __pycache__/ and *.pyc — verified clean.

findings.summary: README and Makefile claim the site is not published, but pages.yml deploys it to GitHub Pages on every master push
findings.evidence: README:3-5 'It is not published to GitHub Pages.'; README:437 'not published anywhere'; Makefile:54 'not published anywhere'. Yet .github/workflows/pages.yml triggers `on: push: branches: [master]` and runs actions/deploy-pages@v4. History: 07c17be (07-06) 'drop GitHub Pages publish' then 10695f1 (07-07) 'add Pages deployment' — docs never caught back up despite the 07-22 'Doc truth sweep' commit.
findings.fix: Single truth sweep: update README intro/commands and Makefile export comment to state the site IS deployed via pages.yml (or delete pages.yml if non-publication is the intent).

findings.summary: Toolchain setup preamble triplicated and the 1.93.1 pin quadruplicated across workflows + rust-toolchain.toml
findings.evidence: Identical 3-step block (checkout@v4, dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master with `toolchain: 1.93.1`, Swatinem/rust-cache@v2) in ci.yml:15-22, pages.yml:24-29, data-release.yml:17-22, each with a 'Keep in sync with rust-toolchain.toml' comment; rust-toolchain.toml:8 `channel = "1.93.1"` is the fourth copy.
findings.fix: Composite action .github/actions/setup-rust that reads the channel from rust-toolchain.toml (or plain rustup, which honors the toml natively); survivor = rust-toolchain.toml as the single pin.

findings.summary: Three different commands write committed data/ artifacts owned by other stages — export, evaluate, and corpus-eval all mutate data/
findings.evidence: site/mod.rs:1622 `std::fs::write("data/novel-words.tsv", &tsv)?` runs on every `export` (hardcoded path, ignores --out); eval.rs:3497 `std::fs::write(crate::calibrate::PATH, ...)` rewrites data/score-calibration.json on every `evaluate`; corpus-eval --fit writes data/corpus-calibration.json (calibrate.rs:21). CI must freeze all three with `git diff --exit-code` (ci.yml:49, ci.yml:101) to keep builds honest.
findings.fix: Make data/ writes explicit opt-in: `export --propose` / `evaluate --refit` (defaulting off in CI, on in the refresh ritual), route through one writer in release.rs, and record ownership per artifact in PIPELINE.md.

findings.summary: 22 committed benchmark snapshots live under gitignored /target/ — `cargo clean` deletes tracked files
findings.evidence: `git ls-files target/` returns 22 files (aspect-pairs.md/.tsv, methodology.md, predictions.csv, ...) while .gitignore line 1 is `/target/` (force-added past the ignore). ci.yml:37 and :101 `git diff --exit-code -- target/eval/...` depend on them; README:212-213 'a committed snapshot is under version control'. `cargo clean` or a fresh clone's target wipe silently deletes tracked content.
findings.fix: Move the committed snapshots to reports/eval/ (writers keep --out target/eval as scratch; the eval commands take --out already, CI passes the new path), leaving /target/ purely ephemeral. Export bytes untouched.

findings.summary: Regression-floor gate scrapes `evaluate` stdout with grep/awk while the same run writes the metric machine-readably
findings.evidence: ci.yml:96-99 `exact=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'exact top-1 [0-9]+\.[0-9]+%' | tail -1 ...)` + awk compare — parsing the human print at eval.rs:298; but evaluate simultaneously writes target/eval/candidate-generation-summary.json containing `runs[]` with an `exact` field per rung (verified by loading the JSON). A wording tweak to the print silently breaks the gate (grep finds nothing, awk compares empty).
findings.fix: Read runs[-1].exact from candidate-generation-summary.json (python3/jq one-liner), keeping the same 39.5 floor.

findings.summary: 13 eval subcommands copy-paste the identical official/out argument pair
findings.evidence: `grep -c DEFAULT_OFFICIAL src/main.rs` = 21, `grep -c 'default_value = "target/eval"'` = 15; ProtoEval (194-199), DeriveEval, MultiwordEval, AspectEval, EvidenceEval, InflectEval, ChecktextEval, Audit, Oracle, SelectEval, RepEval, SynonymEval, Evaluate each repeat the same two #[arg] fields.
findings.fix: One `#[derive(Args)] struct EvalArgs { official, out }` used via `#[command(flatten)]` — clap parse is identical, so behavior-frozen; survivor = the shared struct.

findings.summary: ExtractEnrich arm embeds pipeline logic in the documented 'thin dispatch adapter'
findings.evidence: main.rs:376-399 loads LemmaCorpus, official::load, RawSlavicCorpus (with the absent->empty policy comment), calls enrich::build_wanted, computes totals and prints, before enrich::extract — while README:334 declares main.rs 'thin Clap command parser and library dispatch adapter' and every other arm is a one-line delegate.
findings.fix: Hoist the whole arm into `enrich::run(dir, lemmas, official, out)` next to build_wanted; main.rs keeps the one-line dispatch.

findings.summary: README documents 'interslavic 0.10.0' but the crate pins =0.13.0
findings.evidence: README:513 '(full `tebe`, clitic `tę`, prepositional `njego` — interslavic 0.10.0)' vs Cargo.toml:16 `interslavic = "=0.13.0"` and data/MANIFEST.json:2 `"crate_pin": "=0.13.0"`.
findings.fix: Drop the version number from the prose (the MANIFEST is the pin of record) or state 0.13.0.

findings.summary: README contradicts itself on proposals.html: 'explicitly paused' vs 'Live again since V11'

## Strict-lint adoption cost — slovowiki (single-crate, `interslavic-wiktionary-lab`)

Method: `cargo clippy --all-targets --message-format=json` with the seven strict lints; each warning deduplicated by (lint, file, line) and classified lib vs test by brace-matched `#[cfg(test)]` regions (`src/site/tests.rs` is wholly test via `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` at `src/site/mod.rs:2139`).

### 1. Per-lint counts (608 unique warnings: 306 lib / 302 test)

| Lint | Lib | Test | Total |
|---|---|---|---|
| `indexing_slicing` | 279 | 100 | 379 |
| `unwrap_used` | 17 | 112 | 129 |
| `expect_used` | 6 | 87 | 93 |
| `panic` | 1 | 3 | 4 |
| `unwrap_in_result` | 3 | 0 | 3 |
| `todo` | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| `dbg_macro` | 0 | 0 | 0 |

Per-lint per-file (lib/test), non-zero lib rows first — worst cells:
- `indexing_slicing`: eval.rs 47/0, site/mod.rs 29/0, check.rs 29/37, proto.rs 28/0, consensus.rs 18/0, calibrate.rs 18/0, falsefriends.rs 15/4, site/coverage.rs 13/0, site/english_api.rs 12/23, flavorize.rs 10/0, coincheck.rs 8/0, derive.rs 6/0, site/navigation.rs 6/0, thesaurus.rs 6/0, aspect.rs 5/0, dump.rs 4/0, site/search.rs 4/0, site/special.rs 4/0, orthography.rs 3/0, proto_link.rs 3/0, enrich.rs 3/7, forms.rs 2/4, site/entries.rs 2/0, remainder ≤2.
- `unwrap_used` lib: calibrate.rs 4, check.rs 4, eval.rs 4, site/entries.rs 2, coincheck.rs 1, derive.rs 1, site/english_api.rs 1. Test-heavy: check.rs 35, site/tests.rs 33, release.rs 23.
- `expect_used` lib: check.rs 3, site/mod.rs 2, calibrate.rs 1. Test-heavy: check.rs 44, english_api.rs 17.
- `panic` lib: eval.rs 1 (line 122, fail-fast fixture load). `unwrap_in_result`: check.rs 3.

Top offender files (lib/test/total): check.rs 39/116/155, site/english_api.rs 13/41/54, site/tests.rs 0/53/53, eval.rs 52/0/52, release.rs 1/35/36, site/mod.rs 31/0/31, proto.rs 28/0/28, calibrate.rs 23/4/27, falsefriends.rs 15/7/22, consensus.rs 18/1/19.

### 2. Pedantic scan (810 unique warnings) — 8 most valuable

1. `cast_possible_truncation` (29) — real bug class: usize/u64 counts truncated in stats/percentile code; silently wrong metrics at corpus scale.
2. `cast_sign_loss` (16) — negative-to-unsigned wraps in delta/score arithmetic; same bug family.
3. `match_same_arms` (39) — highest real-bug density here: duplicated arms in morphology/case match tables can hide an unimplemented case pasted from a neighbor.
4. `map_unwrap_or` (83) — largest count; mechanical `.map(..).unwrap_or(..)` → `map_or`, clarifies fallback values in gloss/paradigm pipelines; mostly `--fix`-able.
5. `redundant_closure_for_method_calls` (32) — zero-risk, fully auto-fixable.
6. `uninlined_format_args` (22) — auto-fixable; this codebase is format-string heavy (site generator).
7. `needless_pass_by_value` (7) — real clone cost in per-lemma hot loops.
8. `too_many_lines` (47) — check.rs (2981 lines) and eval.rs (3608 lines) are the maintainability risk the counts point at; warn-and-allow-existing.

Skip as noise: `must_use_candidate` (157, bin-style crate), `cast_precision_loss` (72, corpus counts ≪ 2^52), `missing_errors_doc`/`doc_markdown`/`missing_panics_doc` (doc-only).

### 3. Recommended table

```toml
# Cargo.toml
[workspace]          # already implicit single-package; needed for workspace.lints
[workspace.lints.clippy]
# Zero-cost today — deny to keep it that way
todo = "deny"
dbg_macro = "deny"
# Cheap burn-down (17 lib sites) — warn now, deny after
unwrap_used = "warn"
unwrap_in_result = "warn"
panic = "warn"
# expect-with-message is the sanctioned escape hatch for unwrap burn-down
expect_used = "allow"
# 279 lib sites; batch CLI where a panic = failed build, not an outage.
# Do NOT enable globally yet; burn down per-module (see effort).
indexing_slicing = "allow"
# Pedantic picks
cast_possible_truncation = "warn"
cast_sign_loss = "warn"
match_same_arms = "warn"
map_unwrap_or = "warn"
redundant_closure_for_method_calls = "warn"
uninlined_format_args = "warn"
needless_pass_by_value = "warn"
too_many_lines = "warn"

[lints]
workspace = true
```

Legitimate `#[allow]` scoping:
- **Every `#[cfg(test)] mod`** (and top of `src/site/tests.rs`): `#![allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used, clippy::indexing_slicing, clippy::panic)]` — retires ~302 warnings (unwrap 112, expect 87, indexing 100, panic 3) legitimately; panicking asserts are the point of tests.
- **catch_unwind inflector boundary** (`src/forms.rs:129`, `src/inflect_eval.rs:76/160/241`, `src/check.rs:462`, `src/coincheck.rs:309`, `src/site/entries.rs:1653/1677`): the closures deliberately invoke the panicking upstream `interslavic` inflector and recover blanks; targeted `#[allow]` with a comment on the wrapper fns, not module-wide.
- **`src/eval.rs:122`** fail-fast fixture load (`unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(...))`): intentional CLI fail-fast; `#[allow(clippy::panic)]` with rationale.

### 4. Burn-down effort per lint

| Lint | Lib sites | Effort | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `todo`, `dbg_macro` | 0 | S (free) | deny immediately |
| `panic` | 1 | S | one allow-with-comment |
| `unwrap_in_result` | 3 | S | check.rs, convert to `?` |
| `expect_used` | 6 | S | or keep as allow policy |
| `unwrap_used` | 17 | S–M | ~half day; `?`/expect conversions across 7 files |
| `uninlined_format_args` + `redundant_closure_for_method_calls` | 54 total | S | `cargo clippy --fix` |
| `cast_possible_truncation` + `cast_sign_loss` | 45 total | M | `try_from` or documented `as` per site |
| `map_unwrap_or` | 83 | M | mostly `--fix`-able, review diff |
| `match_same_arms` | 39 | M | human review required — merging morphology arms risks semantic change |
| `indexing_slicing` | 279 | L | multi-week; `get()`/`split_first` rewrites change control flow in eval.rs, check.rs, proto.rs, site/mod.rs hot paths; do per-module, start with site/ (user-facing output) |
| `too_many_lines` | 47 fns | L | check.rs/eval.rs decomposition; treat as ongoing refactor budget |

research.source: tatuylonen/wiktextract + wikitextprocessor (GitHub READMEs) and kaikki.org publication pages (rawdata.html, per-edition error reports, tatuylonen.github.io schema site), researched 2026-07-22

lessons.pattern: Hard two-phase split: raw source -> durable page cache (phase 1, SQLite) vs. extraction/normalization (phase 2, pure function over the cache)
lessons.detail: wikitextprocessor ingests the dump once into SQLite, then all extraction runs against the cache; an existing cache 'eliminat[es] the time needed for Phase 1'. Slovowiki already does the analogue (Wiktextract JSONL -> data/slavic-lemmas.cache.json -> corpus/site), but the lesson is to keep that boundary strict: the committed cache is the only stateful stage, everything downstream stays a pure deterministic function of committed inputs.
lessons.applicability: Direct fit — this IS the committed-cache/offline-build rule; formalizing it (never let site/corpus code peek at raw JSONL) protects determinism. SQLite itself is unneeded at 46k lemmas; committed JSON is fine.

lessons.pattern: Single-page reprocessing for the dev loop (--page, --override, --pages-dir) instead of incremental builds
lessons.detail: wiktextract lets you re-extract one page from the cache ('wiktwords --db-path ... --page page_title') and test a locally modified page before a full run. Slovowiki has no per-lemma/per-cognate-set debug entry point; adding a `--word`/`--set` flag that runs the full pipeline for one entry from the caches would make rule tweaks inspectable in milliseconds.
lessons.applicability: Fits perfectly: pure function over committed caches, no state, no network. Cheap at 18.5k/46k scale.

lessons.pattern: Full rebuilds as the publication model; incrementality only at dev level
lessons.detail: kaikki re-extracts everything on cadence ('updated regularly, usually at least once a week') rather than diffing published JSONL — even at 23 GB output. Incremental machinery is deliberately absent; correctness comes from rerunning the whole deterministic pipeline against a pinned dump.
lessons.applicability: Strong fit — slovowiki's builds are seconds-to-minutes; incremental site generation would add nondeterminism risk for zero gain. Keep full rebuilds, use lesson 2 for iteration speed.

lessons.pattern: Provenance stamping on every published artifact: source dump date + exact tool commit hashes
lessons.detail: kaikki pages/footers record 'the enwiktionary dump dated 2026-07-06', processing completion date, and commit hashes of both wiktextract and wikitextprocessor, 'enabling precise reproduction'. Slovowiki's data/MANIFEST.json and refresh-changelog.md are the natural home: record source dump date, sha256 of each committed cache, and the generator git commit; also emit it into the static site footer.
lessons.applicability: Perfect fit — it is the reproducibility discipline the house rules imply but that a manifest can enforce mechanically (build fails if cache hash != manifest).

lessons.pattern: Diagnostics as structured data, published as first-class static release artifacts
lessons.detail: wikitextprocessor collects error/warning/debug messages as lists of dicts (page title, section, path of templates/modules, sortid), and kaikki renders them into browsable static error pages grouped by message kind with per-page links and counts, shipped alongside each dataset. Slovowiki's validators (tools/validate-*.py/.mjs) print to CI; instead emit a build-diagnostics JSON (rule id, severity, entry, detail) and render a static /errors or /audit page grouped by rule.
lessons.applicability: Fits static-only output exactly; at 18.5k entries the report is tiny and doubles as a curation worklist. Deterministic since it derives from the same committed inputs.

lessons.pattern: Publish machine-readable schemas generated from the typed models, with schema-diffing across editions
lessons.detail: Non-English wiktextract editions ship JSON Schemas at tatuylonen.github.io (pinned to a commit, with a 'Compare JSON schemas' view); the English edition's lack of one is a documented pain point. Slovowiki can derive JSON Schema from its serde types (schemars) for cache files and the 14-column search.json row, commit them, and validate caches against them at load — extending the existing corpus-calibration load-time validation.
lessons.applicability: Fits: deterministic codegen, offline, and it hardens the producer/consumer contract for the 569k-record index whose rows are positional arrays (currently documented only in code).

lessons.pattern: Additive-only, unversioned schema evolution ('more fields may be added in the future')
lessons.detail: wiktextract evolves its JSONL by adding fields with no version number or migration story; consumers must tolerate unknown keys. That works for a loose public dataset but is their weakest discipline — schema drift is only discoverable by diffing output.
lessons.applicability: Reject for slovowiki: it controls both producer and consumer, so it should instead put an explicit schema/version field in MANIFEST.json and each cache, and fail loudly on mismatch rather than tolerating drift silently.

lessons.pattern: Split large published datasets into per-subset downloads so consumers pull only what they need
lessons.detail: kaikki shards the 23 GB extraction into per-language, per-word, and error files rather than forcing the monolith. Slovowiki's analogue is the 569k-record search.json: shard it into prefix/bucket files fetched lazily by the client, keeping first-paint payload small while remaining fully static.
lessons.applicability: Fits static-only hosting (plain file fetches, no server); deterministic sharding by folded first char/bucket hash. Worth it only if search.json size is actually hurting load time — measure first with tools/search-perf.mjs.

research.source: /Users/kisaczka/Desktop/code/interslavic-rs (local checkout; workspace now at 0.14.0, ahead of slovowiki's =0.13.0 pin). Examined: crates/xtask/src/main.rs, crates/interslavic/src/fingerprint.rs, INTEGRATION.md, CHANGELOG.md policy header, tests/variant_order.rs, tools/compare-latest-sonic.js, workspace Cargo.toml (coallescor excluded), lint config (none exists).

lessons.pattern: Whole-output fingerprint pinned in a unit test (inline dep-free FNV-1a over a canonical lemma\tcell\tform dump; failure message names the exact remediation commands and the deliberate-update protocol)
lessons.detail: src/fingerprint.rs streams every paradigm cell in stable BTreeSet/fixed-iteration order into one string, hashes it, and pins the u64 as EXPECTED_FINGERPRINT with cell count and version in the comment. Any output change anywhere fails one test with a message telling you to run diff-fingerprint and update the constant only in a commit that lists the delta.
lessons.applicability: Fits perfectly: slovowiki's exports are already deterministic/offline, and it has scattered byte-identical guards (dump.rs, enrich.rs) but no single number that fails when ANY output moves. Hash the canonical site export (e.g. search.json + entry-page corpus) or the generated-forms dump; FNV-1a inline keeps it dependency-free.

lessons.pattern: dump-paradigms + diff-fingerprint pair: a canonical whole-output dump command plus a keyed differ, so the changelog entry for any fingerprint change is mechanically enumerated (changed/removed/added per cell)
lessons.detail: xtask dump-paradigms writes target/paradigm-fingerprint/<name>.tsv (named dumps survive for diffing across trees); diff-fingerprint loads both into BTreeMap<lemma\tcell, form> and prints the exact delta with counts. The fingerprint test and the dump share one corpus generator so they cannot disagree.
lessons.applicability: Fits: pairs with the fingerprint test above and turns 'the hash changed' from a mystery into an enumerated diff. Implement as slovowiki CLI subcommands (dump-output / diff-output) rather than xtask — slovowiki's binary is already its task runner via the Makefile.

lessons.pattern: cargo xtask workspace task runner (Rust binary crate dispatching refresh-data/check-all/etc.)
lessons.detail: crates/xtask is a thin Command-spawning dispatcher; check-all = fmt --check + workspace tests + generated-file currency check.
lessons.applicability: Conflicts with slovowiki's shape: one flat crate whose Makefile already dispatches to `cargo run --` subcommands — adding a workspace member just to run tasks is ceremony with no gain. The one idea worth stealing is a single `check` target that chains fmt + tests + the fingerprint/manifest gates.

lessons.pattern: INTEGRATION.md as a downstream contract document: everything a consumer learned the hard way, in one root-level file — citation-form conventions, output-order contracts, which fields are truth, a worked CI-tested example
lessons.detail: 163 lines covering byform-order-is-API, clean vs _raw, None-means-no-such-form semantics, and notably the folded-key homograph rule for interpreting slovowiki's own check-text output — that rule about slovowiki's API is currently documented only in the UPSTREAM repo.
lessons.applicability: Strong fit: slovowiki has real downstream consumers (interslavic-rs's phrase-check gate calls `check-text --json`; data-vN tags are pinned externally) but no contract doc. Write one covering the check-text JSON schema and status semantics, the folded-key/lemmas-field rule, data-release pinning via MANIFEST.json, and which exports are byte-stable.

lessons.pattern: CHANGELOG policy header 'variant order is API' + a dedicated fence test (tests/variant_order.rs) pinning one representative output per class, updatable only with a changelog entry
lessons.detail: The policy line sits at the top of CHANGELOG.md, names the enforcing test, and defines reordering as breaking because consumers bless first-variant outputs into expectations.
lessons.applicability: Adapt: slovowiki has no CHANGELOG at all, but it does expose observable orderings (candidate ranking, search.json row schema/order) that downstream and blessed tests depend on. Add a CHANGELOG (or CONTRIBUTING section) with an explicit ordering-is-API policy line plus a small fence test per ordered surface; the manifest guards bytes but not the stated policy.

lessons.pattern: Parity harness with per-category minimum compatibility rates and dated inline justifications for every threshold change ('upstream data drift, not a regression — relaxed one notch')
lessons.detail: tools/compare-latest-sonic.js pins MIN_COMPATIBLE_RATES per POS (noun 0.9998, verb 0.95, pronoun 1.0) and each relaxation carries a dated comment explaining the diagnosis. But the harness itself fetches a live Google Sheet and git-fetches an external checkout at run time.
lessons.applicability: The threshold-band-with-dated-rationale discipline fits slovowiki's eval/aspect-eval/corpus-calibration bands and is worth copying wholesale; the live-network structure conflicts with deterministic/offline house rules — slovowiki's version must run against pinned local reference data only.

lessons.pattern: Cross-repo reciprocal gate kept manual/local, never CI: xtask phrase-check locates a sibling checkout via env var (SLOVOWIKI_DIR, default ../slovowiki), runs goldens through the OTHER project's independent checker, and documents interpretation caveats at the gate itself
lessons.detail: It also handles the toolchain seam correctly (plain `cargo`, not $CARGO, so slovowiki's rust-toolchain.toml wins) and writes the failing JSON report to a stable path.
lessons.applicability: Fits as the mirror image: slovowiki could add a local-only gate that renders its own golden sentences/forms through the pinned interslavic crate (already a dependency, so this one is even CI-safe and offline). Keep any variant needing a sibling checkout out of CI, per upstream's precedent.

lessons.pattern: Quarantine dead/prototype code outside the workspace via Cargo `exclude` (coallescor is an old prototype excluded so fmt/test/check-all never touch it)
lessons.detail: Workspace Cargo.toml: members = crates/* + experiments/*, exclude = ["coallescor"]. The crates/core-vs-data split (dependency-free rules crate + generated-metadata crate + extractor + check-registry currency check) is the rest of the layout.
lessons.applicability: The exclude trick is moot for slovowiki (single flat crate; its tools/ scripts are not Cargo members anyway). The core/data crate split solves a library-consumer problem slovowiki does not have — its consumers use the CLI and data tags, not a lib API. The check-registry idea (verify generated files are current) is impractical for slovowiki since regeneration needs the 23GB dump; MANIFEST.json sha256 verification already covers integrity.

lessons.pattern: Lint configuration: none exists upstream — no [workspace.lints], no clippy config, no lint attributes; the only style gate is `cargo fmt --all -- --check` inside xtask check-all
lessons.applicability: Nothing to adopt; interslavic-rs offers no precedent here. If slovowiki wants a lint gate it should decide independently (e.g. clippy in `make check`), not copy upstream.

lessons.pattern: Failure messages and doc comments that encode the operational protocol: the fingerprint assert names the exact commands to run and requires the update to land 'deliberately, in a commit whose message lists the delta'; the pinned constant's comment records cell count and version
lessons.applicability: Pure fit, zero cost: slovowiki's byte-identical and manifest guards should tell the developer the precise regenerate/diff/verify commands and the changelog obligation in the assert message itself, matching its existing heavily-annotated house style.

research.source: Web research: DVC pipeline docs (dvc.yaml/dvc.lock), reproducible-builds.org + Debian ReproducibleBuilds (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH, .buildinfo, variation testing), Unicode CLDR build/consistency-check design docs, FreeDict make/TEI build system, kaikki.org wiktextract publishing — judged against slovowiki's Makefile, data/MANIFEST.json, and docs/DATA-REFRESH.md

lessons.pattern: Stage = pure function of DECLARED deps/outs; the DAG is derived from declarations, never narrated
lessons.detail: DVC stages list cmd/deps/outs and the pipeline DAG falls out of outs-of-A matching deps-of-B. Slovowiki's DAG lives in Makefile comments ('run extract-enrich AFTER extract-lemmas') on .PHONY targets, so nothing enforces order or freshness. Converting cache files to real Make file targets (extract-enrich depends on the two cache files) gets enforced ordering, skip-if-fresh, and a machine-readable DAG (`make -Bnd`) from ~15 lines of Makefile — no new tools.
lessons.applicability: Fits perfectly: Make is already the entry point, file-target deps are deterministic and offline, and it turns prose ordering rules into checked ones at zero dependency cost.

lessons.pattern: Lock-file provenance: record per-artifact producing command + input hashes, not just output hashes
lessons.detail: dvc.lock stores, per stage, the command plus hashes of every dep and every out — so 'was this artifact built from these exact inputs by this exact step?' is answerable offline. Slovowiki's MANIFEST.json is a flat file list; it proves integrity but not lineage. Adding a small `stages` section (stage name, input paths+hashes, output paths) to the existing data-manifest command makes the 'each stage is a pure function of declared inputs' rule verifiable instead of aspirational.
lessons.applicability: Fits: extends the existing hand-rolled MANIFEST.json/data-manifest verb; deterministic JSON, no runtime, one maintainer can keep ~6 stage entries honest because CI already runs `data-manifest` in verify mode.

lessons.pattern: DVC the tool (content-addressed cache, remotes, incremental skip engine)
lessons.detail: DVC's machinery exists to avoid re-running expensive stages and to sync large blobs to remote storage. Slovowiki's full pipeline (minus the one-time 23GB dump extraction) rebuilds fast, caches are committed to git, and remotes violate the offline rule; an incremental-skip engine also weakens the strongest guarantee slovowiki has — full rebuild + byte-identical compare.
lessons.applicability: Conflicts: Python dependency, network remotes, and cache indirection are enterprise machinery that pays no rent at 38k lines with git-committed data. Take the lock-file schema idea (above), not the tool.

lessons.pattern: buildinfo-style provenance stamp on the OUTPUT artifact (site/build-info.json)
lessons.detail: Debian ships a .buildinfo per package recording exactly what produced the binary, kept separate from the artifact so verification is decoupled from delivery. Slovowiki's manifest covers data/ inputs but a deployed site/ carries no record of which data_release, git commit, or rustc produced it. Emitting site/build-info.json {data_release, source commit, rustc -V, manifest sha256} during export makes any deployed copy traceable; all values are committed/pinned, so the stamp is itself deterministic.
lessons.applicability: Fits: pure function of committed state (rust-toolchain.toml pins rustc), ~20 lines in export, and it closes the one provenance gap the refresh/release ritual doesn't cover — artifacts that left the repo.

lessons.pattern: SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH principle: exactly one committed time source, wall clock banned from all output paths
lessons.detail: Reproducible-builds' core move on timestamps: any date in an artifact derives from a declared source-controlled value (Debian: the changelog), never now(). Slovowiki equivalent: if any page, TSV, or eval report wants a date, it must come from the refresh-changelog/data_release entry. Worth codifying as a house rule plus a CI grep for SystemTime::now/chrono::Utc::now in export paths, since the double-export check only catches clock use at second granularity by luck.
lessons.applicability: Fits directly — it is the canonical formulation of a rule slovowiki mostly follows implicitly; the grep-lint makes regressions impossible instead of unlikely.

lessons.pattern: Verify by rebuilding under DELIBERATE variation, not just twice in place; diffoscope for triage
lessons.detail: Debian's CI rebuilds with varied build path, locale, timezone, and filesystem ordering to flush latent nondeterminism; a plain A/B rebuild in the same environment misses path- and locale-dependence. Slovowiki's byte-identical double export already catches HashMap-order bugs (Rust's per-process SipHash seed varies) but runs from the same cwd/locale. Cheap upgrade: second export from a different absolute path with LC_ALL varied. When a compare fails, diffoscope (offline CLI) localizes the diff far faster than diff -r.
lessons.applicability: Fits: shell-only change to an existing check; diffoscope is an ad-hoc offline debugging tool, not pipeline machinery. Skip Debian's VM-farm scale — one varied rebuild in CI is the right dose.

lessons.pattern: Stable-ordering commandment: sort at every serialization boundary, BTreeMap-by-default
lessons.detail: The single most common determinism bug class in the reproducible-builds corpus is unordered inputs/outputs (readdir order, hash-map iteration). Codify as a written rule + review checklist item: any loop that writes bytes iterates a sorted or BTree collection, and directory reads are sorted before use. Slovowiki passes today (double export proves it) but the rule keeps new code from relying on the check as its only defense.
lessons.applicability: Fits: zero-cost convention, Rust makes it easy (BTreeMap, sort_unstable before emit), and it converts an emergent property into a stated invariant a one-maintainer project can hold in review.

lessons.pattern: CLDR: validation is a NAMED PIPELINE STAGE over the full artifact, distinct from unit tests, with committed exception lists
lessons.detail: CLDR runs ConsoleCheckCLDR (full-data sweep of cross-field invariants: every language has a default script, every locale has population data, etc.) as its own gating build step, separate from code unit tests; fuzzy invariants stay strict by carrying an explicit committed exception list rather than loosening the check. Slovowiki's three site validators already follow the stage shape — the importable pieces are (a) maintain an enumerable catalog of cross-artifact invariants (every search row's id resolves to a page, every official lemma appears exactly once, every rendered page is reachable from the index) and (b) when an invariant has legitimate outliers, commit an exceptions file so the check never gets weakened globally.
lessons.applicability: Fits the house rules exactly (deterministic full-corpus sweeps, no sampling, offline); exception-lists-over-loosened-checks is the same philosophy as slovowiki's 'floor is re-argued in the changelog, never silently'.

lessons.pattern: FreeDict hub-and-spoke: one canonical intermediate, schema-validated at the stage boundary, all outputs pure converters from it
lessons.detail: FreeDict funnels every importer into canonical TEI, validates it against a committed RELAX NG schema, then derives all distribution formats from that single validated hub. Slovowiki's hub is its cache JSONs; full XML-schema tooling would be dead weight, but the boundary-validation idea lands as: serde with deny_unknown_fields on every cache struct plus load-time invariant checks that fail loud (the corpus-calibration band validation added recently is exactly this pattern — extend it to all committed caches).
lessons.applicability: Adapt, not adopt: RELAX NG/XML is the wrong substrate for Rust+JSON, but 'the hub artifact is validated the moment a stage loads it, not when a downstream symptom appears' fits deterministic/offline and costs a few lines per cache.

lessons.pattern: kaikki.org: ship the machine-readable corpus as a first-class site artifact, carrying the same release identity as the pages
lessons.detail: kaikki publishes the raw wiktextract JSONL for download alongside the browsable dictionary, making the site self-describing for downstream consumers. Slovowiki already emits search.json and novel-words.tsv; the lesson is to treat them as advertised, stable, data_release-stamped outputs (linked from the site, covered by the manifest/provenance stamp) rather than internal byproducts — consumers then pin data-vN exactly as docs/DATA-REFRESH.md intends.
lessons.applicability: Fits: static files only, no API, no service; it extends the existing release ritual's 'consumers pin data-vN' promise to the data files themselves at near-zero cost.

MISSED ITEMS (whole-repo view)

1. **Two unclaimed areas hold the two largest files.** No auditor owns site/* (~11.6k lines: navigation.rs 2122, english_api.rs 1895, entries.rs 1833, coverage.rs 1437, special.rs 1114, search.rs 724, mod.rs 2140) or eval.rs (3608, the largest file in the repo). All four reports cite them only as counterparties. Also unaudited: falsefriends.rs (1249), calibrate.rs (394), generator.rs, inflect_eval.rs, orthography.rs internals, main.rs wiring.

2. **Calibrator lifecycle is unowned and the generation report understates it.** Generation calls corpus-calibration.json "the one generation artifact produced by one command and consumed by another." Whole-repo there are THREE cross-command committed artifacts: (a) data/score-calibration.json written by `evaluate` (eval.rs:3497) and read by export (site/mod.rs:86, 2091); (b) data/corpus-calibration.json written by `corpus-eval --fit` (calibrate.rs:21) and read by export (site/mod.rs:469); (c) data/novel-words.tsv written by export, read by check/coincheck. Crucially, CI freshness-guards (a) and (c) (`git diff --exit-code -- data/novel-words.tsv data/score-calibration.json`, ci.yml:49, again :101) but **corpus-calibration.json has no CI regeneration guard** — only the MANIFEST hash pin, which checks tree-vs-manifest, not fit-vs-code. It can silently go stale relative to corpus::build_sets changes; the recent band-validation commit checks shape, not freshness.

3. **CI already machine-checks the frozen-export invariant** — double export + full tree sha256 diff (ci.yml:45-48) plus the artifact git-diff guards. Nobody noted this; it means "byte-identical" proofs for every proposed refactor are nearly free, which changes the sequencing calculus (code-motion commits are cheap to verify).

The surface report's "five tokenizers" map missed this fourth cross-module reach (site→falsefriends), and missed that dump::gloss_tokens has ~14 consumers across eval, proto_link (production-path scoring), thesaurus, and site/mod.rs — it is already the de facto shared tokenizer, so the gloss.rs consolidation must include it.
